text.NRSV:
1:1 Happy are they who have not walked in the counsel of the wicked, nor lingered in the way of sinners, nor sat in the seats of the scornful!
1:2 Their delight is in the law of the Lord, and they meditate on his law day and night.
1:3 They are like trees planted by streams of water, bearing fruit in due season, with leaves that do not wither; everything they do shall prosper.
1:4 It is not so with the wicked; they are like chaff which the wind blows away.
1:5 Therefore the wicked shall not stand upright when judgment comes, nor the sinner in the council of the righteous.
1:6 For the Lord knows the way of the righteous, but the way of the wicked is doomed.
text.Coverdale:
1:1 Blessed is the man who has not walked in the counsel of the wicked, *
      nor lingered in the way of sinners,
      nor sat in the seats of the scornful!
1:2 His delight is in the law of the LORD, *
      and he meditates on his law day and night.
1:3 He is like a tree planted by streams of water,
      bearing fruit in due season, with leaves that do not wither; *
      everything he does shall prosper.
1:4 It is not so with the wicked; *
      they are like chaff which the wind blows away.
1:5 Therefore the wicked shall not stand upright when judgment comes, *
      nor the sinner in the council of the righteous.
1:6 For the LORD knows the way of the righteous, *
      but the way of the wicked is doomed.
text.KJV:
1:1 Blessed is the man that walketh not in the counsel of the ungodly, nor standeth in the way of sinners, nor sitteth in the seat of the scornful.
1:2 But his delight is in the law of the LORD; and in his law doth he meditate day and night.
1:3 And he shall be like a tree planted by the rivers of water, that bringeth forth his fruit in his season; his leaf also shall not wither; and whatsoever he doeth shall prosper.
1:4 The ungodly are not so: but are like the chaff which the wind driveth away.
1:5 Therefore the ungodly shall not stand in the judgment, nor sinners in the congregation of the righteous.
1:6 For the LORD knoweth the way of the righteous: but the way of the ungodly shall perish.
text.JPS:
1:1 Happy is the man who has not followed the counsel of the wicked, or taken the path of sinners, or joined the company of the insolent;
1:2 rather, the teaching of the LORD is his delight, and he studies His teaching day and night.
1:3 He is like a tree planted beside streams of water, which yields its fruit in season, whose foliage never fades, and whatever it produces thrives.
1:4 Not so the wicked; they are like chaff that wind blows away.
1:5 Therefore the wicked will not survive judgment, nor will sinners thrive in the assembly of the righteous.
1:6 For the LORD cherishes the way of the righteous, but the way of the wicked leads to ruin.

text.NRSV:
2:1 Why are the nations in an uproar? Why do the peoples mutter empty threats?
2:2 The kings of the earth rise up in revolt, and the princes plot together, against the Lord and against his Anointed.
2:3 'Let us break their yoke,' they say; 'let us cast off their bonds from us.'
2:4 He whose throne is in heaven is laughing; the Lord has them in derision.
2:5 Then he speaks to them in his wrath, and his rage fills them with terror.
2:6 'I myself have set my king upon my holy hill of Zion.'
2:7 Let me announce the decree of the Lord: he said to me, 'You are my Son; this day have I begotten you.
2:8 Ask of me, and I will give you the nations for your inheritance and the ends of the earth for your possession.
2:9 You shall crush them with an iron rod and shatter them like a piece of pottery.'
2:10 And now, you kings, be wise; be warned, you rulers of the earth.
2:11 Submit to the Lord with fear, and with trembling bow before him;
2:12 Lest he be angry and you perish; for his wrath is quickly kindled.
2:13 Happy are they all who take refuge in him!

text.KJV:
2:1 Why do the heathen rage, and the people imagine a vain thing?
2:2 The kings of the earth set themselves, and the rulers take counsel together, against the LORD, and against his anointed, saying,
2:3 Let us break their bands asunder, and cast away their cords from us.
2:4 He that sitteth in the heavens shall laugh: the Lord shall have them in derision.
2:5 Then shall he speak unto them in his wrath, and vex them in his sore displeasure.
2:6 Yet have I set my king upon my holy hill of Zion.
2:7 I will declare the decree: the Lord hath said unto me, Thou art my Son; this day have I begotten thee.
2:8 Ask of me, and I shall give thee the heathen for thine inheritance, and the uttermost parts of the earth for thy possession.
2:9 Thou shalt break them with a rod of iron; thou shalt dash them in pieces like a potter's vessel.
2:10 Be wise now therefore, O ye kings: be instructed, ye judges of the earth.
2:11 Serve the LORD with fear, and rejoice with trembling.
2:12 Kiss the Son, lest he be angry, and ye perish from the way, when his wrath is kindled but a little. Blessed are all they that put their trust in him.
text.JPS:
2:1 Why do nations assemble, and peoples plot in vain?
2:2 Kings of the earth take their stand, and regents intrigue together against the LORD and against His anointed:
2:3 "Let us break the cords of their yoke, shake off their ropes from us!"
2:4 He who is enthroned in heaven laughs; the Lord mocks them.
2:5 Then He speaks to them in His anger; His fury terrifies them:
2:6 "I have installed My king on Zion, My holy mountain!"
2:7 Let me tell of the decree: the LORD said to me, "You are My son; I have fathered you this day.
2:8 Ask it of Me, and I will make the nations your domain; the ends of the earth, your possession.
2:9 You can smash them with an iron mace, shatter them like potter's ware."
2:10 So now, O kings, be prudent; accept discipline, you rulers of the earth.
2:11 Serve the LORD in awe; tremble with fright,
2:12 pay homage in desire, lest He be angered, and your way be doomed in a trice; for His anger flares up in a moment. Happy are all who take refuge in Him.

text.NRSV:
3:1 Lord, how many adversaries I have; how many there are who rise up against me.
3:2 How many there are who say of me, 'There is no help for him in his God.'
3:3 But you, O Lord, are a shield about me; you are my glory, the one who lifts up my head.
3:4 I call aloud upon the Lord, and he answers me from his holy hill.
3:5 I lie down and go to sleep; I wake again, because the Lord sustains me.
3:6 I do not fear the multitudes of people who set themselves against me all around.
3:7 Rise up, O Lord; set me free, O my God; surely you will strike all my enemies across the face, you will break the teeth of the wicked.
3:8 Deliverance belongs to the Lord. Your blessing be upon your people!
text.Coverdale:

text.KJV:
3:1 LORD, how are they increased that trouble me! many are they that rise up against me.
3:2 Many there be which say of my soul, There is no help for him in God. Selah.
3:3 But thou, O LORD, art a shield for me; my glory, and the lifter up of mine head.
3:4 I cried unto the LORD with my voice, and he heard me out of his holy hill. Selah.
3:5 I laid me down and slept; I awaked; for the LORD sustained me.
3:6 I will not be afraid of ten thousands of people, that have set themselves against me round about.
3:7 Arise, O LORD; save me, O my God: for thou hast smitten all mine enemies upon the cheek bone; thou hast broken the teeth of the ungodly.
3:8 Salvation belongeth unto the LORD: thy blessing is upon thy people. Selah.
text.JPS:
3:1 A psalm of David, when he fled from his son Absalom.
3:2 O LORD, my foes are so many! Many are those who rise up against me;
3:3 many are those who say of me, "There is no deliverance for him through God." Selah.
3:4 But You, O LORD, are a shield about me, my glory, He who holds my head high.
3:5 I cry aloud to the LORD, and He answers me from His holy mountain. Selah.
3:6 I lie down and sleep; I awake, for the LORD sustains me.
3:7 I am not afraid of myriads of people who beset me on every side.
3:8 Rise up, O LORD! Deliver me, O my God! For You slap all my enemies on the cheek; You break the teeth of the wicked.
3:9 Deliverance comes from the LORD; Your blessing be upon Your people! Selah.

text.NRSV:
4:1 Answer me when I call, O God, defender of my cause; you set me free when I am hard-pressed; have mercy on me and hear my prayer.
4:2 You mortals, how long will you dishonor my glory; how long will you worship dumb idols and run after false gods?
4:3 Know that the Lord does wonders for the faithful; when I call upon the Lord, he will hear me.
4:4 Tremble, then, and do not sin; speak to your heart in silence upon your bed.
4:5 Offer the appointed sacrifices and put your trust in the Lord.
4:6 Many are saying, 'Oh, that we might see better times!' Lift up the light of your countenance upon us, O Lord.
4:7 You have put gladness in my heart, more than when grain and wine and oil increase.
4:8 I lie down in peace; at once I fall asleep; for only you, Lord, make me dwell in safety.
text.Coverdale:

      you set me free when I am hard-pressed;
      have mercy on me and hear my prayer.

      how long will you worship dumb idols
      and run after false gods?"
4:3 Know that the LORD does wonders for the faithful; *
      when I call upon the LORD, he will hear me.
4:4 Tremble, then, and do not sin; *
      speak to your heart in silence upon your bed.
4:5 Offer the appointed sacrifices *

4:6 Many are saying, "Oh, that we might see better times!" *

text.KJV:
4:1 Hear me when I call, O God of my righteousness: thou hast enlarged me when I was in distress; have mercy upon me, and hear my prayer.
4:2 O ye sons of men, how long will ye turn my glory into shame? how long will ye love vanity, and seek after leasing? Selah.
4:3 But know that the LORD hath set apart him that is godly for himself: the LORD will hear when I call unto him.
4:4 Stand in awe, and sin not: commune with your own heart upon your bed, and be still. Selah.
4:5 Offer the sacrifices of righteousness, and put your trust in the LORD.
4:6 There be many that say, Who will shew us any good? LORD, lift thou up the light of thy countenance upon us.
4:7 Thou hast put gladness in my heart, more than in the time that their corn and their wine increased.
4:8 I will both lay me down in peace, and sleep: for thou, LORD, only makest me dwell in safety.
text.JPS:
4:1 For the leader; with instrumental music. A psalm of David.
4:2 When I cry, answer me, O God, my vindicator! You freed me from distress; have mercy on me and hear my prayer.
4:3 You men, how long will my glory be mocked? How long will you love vanity, chase after lies? Selah.
4:4 Know that the LORD desires the faithful; the LORD hears when I cry to Him.
4:5 Tremble, and do not sin; ponder it on your bed, and sigh. Selah.
4:6 Offer sacrifices in righteousness, and trust in the LORD.
4:7 Many say, "O for a good sign!" Flash the light of Your countenance upon us, O LORD.
4:8 You put joy into my heart when their grain and wine abound.
4:9 Safe and sound, I lie down and sleep, for You alone, O LORD, keep me secure.

text.NRSV:
5:1 Give ear to my words, O Lord; consider my meditation.
5:2 Hearken to my cry for help, my King and my God, for I make my prayer to you.
5:3 In the morning, Lord, you hear my voice; early in the morning I make my appeal and watch for you.
5:4 For you are not a God who takes pleasure in wickedness, and evil cannot dwell with you.
5:5 Braggarts cannot stand in your sight; you hate all those who work wickedness.
5:6 You destroy those who speak lies; the bloodthirsty and deceitful, O Lord, you abhor.
5:7 But as for me, through the greatness of your mercy, I will go into your house; I will bow down toward your holy temple in awe of you.
5:8 Lead me, O Lord, in your righteousness, because of those who lie in wait for me; make your way straight before me.
5:9 For there is no truth in their mouth; there is destruction within them.
5:10 Their throat is an open grave; they flatter with their tongue.
5:11 Declare them guilty, O God; let them fall by their own devices.
5:12 Because of their many transgressions cast them out, for they have rebelled against you.
5:13 But let all who take refuge in you be glad; let them ever sing out their joy with your loving care.
5:14 Shake them that they may be joyful in you, those who love your Name.
5:15 For you, O Lord, will bless the righteous; you will defend them with your favor as with a shield.

text.KJV:
5:1 Give ear to my words, O LORD, consider my meditation.
5:2 Hearken unto the voice of my cry, my King, and my God: for unto thee will I pray.
5:3 My voice shalt thou hear in the morning, O LORD; in the morning will I direct my prayer unto thee, and will look up.
5:4 For thou art not a God that hath pleasure in wickedness: neither shall evil dwell with thee.
5:5 The foolish shall not stand in thy sight: thou hatest all workers of iniquity.
5:6 Thou shalt destroy them that speak leasing: the LORD will abhor the bloody and deceitful man.
5:7 But as for me, I will come into your house in the multitude of thy mercy: and in thy fear will I worship toward thy holy temple.
5:8 Lead me, O LORD, in your righteousness because of mine enemies; make thy way straight before my face.
5:9 For there is no faithfulness in their mouth; their inward part is very wickedness; their throat is an open sepulchre; they flatter with their tongue.
5:10 Destroy thou them, O God; let them fall by their own counsels; cast them out in the multitude of their transgressions; for they have rebelled against thee.
5:11 But let all those that put their trust in thee rejoice: let them ever shout for joy, because thou defendest them: let them also that love thy name be joyful in thee.
5:12 For thou, LORD, wilt bless the righteous; with favour wilt thou compass him as with a shield.
text.JPS:
5:1 For the leader; on wind instruments. A psalm of David.
5:2 Hear my words, O LORD; consider my utterance.
5:3 Listen to my cry for help, my King and my God, for I pray to You.
5:4 Hear my voice each morning, O LORD; each morning I lay my case before You, and wait.
5:5 For You are not a God who desires wickedness; evil cannot abide with You;
5:6 wanton men cannot stand in Your presence. You detest all evildoers;
5:7 You destroy those who speak lies; the LORD abhors bloodthirsty and deceitful men.
5:8 But I, through Your abundant love, enter Your house; I bow down in awe toward Your holy temple.
5:9 O LORD, lead me in Your righteousness because of my watchful foes; make Your way straight before me.
5:10 For there is no sincerity on their lips; their heart is malice itself; their throat is an open grave; their tongue slippery.
5:11 Condemn them, O God; let them fall by their own devices; cast them out for their many crimes, for they have defied You.
5:12 But let all who take refuge in You rejoice, ever sing for joy! Protect them, and let those who love Your name exult in You.
5:13 For You, O LORD, bless the righteous; You surround them with favor as with a shield.

text.NRSV:
6:1 O Lord, do not rebuke me in your anger, or discipline me in your wrath.
6:2 Be gracious to me, O Lord, for I am languishing; O Lord, heal me, for my bones are shaking with terror.
6:3 My soul also is shaking with terror; how long, O Lord, how long?
6:4 Turn, O Lord, save my life; deliver me for the sake of your steadfast love.
6:5 For in death there is no remembrance of you; in Sheol who can give you praise?
6:6 I am weary with my moaning; every night I flood my bed with tears; I drench my couch with my weeping.
6:7 My eyes waste away because of grief; they grow weak because of all my foes.
6:8 Depart from me, all you workers of evil, for the Lord has heard the sound of my weeping.
6:9 The Lord has heard my supplication; the Lord accepts my prayer.
6:10 All my enemies shall be ashamed and struck with terror; they shall turn back, and in a moment be put to shame.
text.Coverdale:
6:1 LORD, do not rebuke me in your anger; *
      do not punish me in your wrath.
6:2 Have pity on me, LORD, for I am weak; *
      heal me, LORD, for my bones are racked.
6:3 My spirit shakes with terror; *
      how long, O LORD, how long?
6:4 Turn, O LORD, and deliver me; *
      save me for your mercy's sake.
6:5 For in death no one remembers you; *
      and who will give you thanks in the grave?
6:6 I grow weary because of my groaning; *
      every night I drench my bed
      and flood my couch with tears.
6:7 My eyes are wasted with grief *
      and worn away because of all my enemies.
6:8 Depart from me, all evildoers, *
      for the LORD has heard the sound of my weeping.
6:9 The LORD has heard my supplication; *
      the LORD accepts my prayer.
6:10 All my enemies shall be confounded and quake with fear; *
      they shall turn back and suddenly be put to shame.
text.KJV:
6:1 O LORD, rebuke me not in thine anger, neither chasten me in thy hot displeasure.
6:2 Have mercy upon me, O LORD; for I am weak: O LORD, heal me; for my bones are vexed.
6:3 My soul is also sore vexed: but thou, O LORD, how long?
6:4 Return, O LORD, deliver my soul: oh save me for thy mercies' sake.
6:5 For in death there is no remembrance of thee: in the grave who shall give thee thanks?
6:6 I am weary with my groaning; all the night make I my bed to swim; I water my couch with my tears.
6:7 Mine eye is consumed because of grief; it waxeth old because of all mine enemies.
6:8 Depart from me, all ye workers of iniquity; for the LORD hath heard the voice of my weeping.
6:9 The LORD hath heard my supplication; the LORD will receive my prayer.
6:10 Let all mine enemies be ashamed and sore vexed: let them return and be ashamed suddenly.
text.JPS:
6:1 For the leader; with instrumental music on the sheminith. A psalm of David.
6:2 O LORD, do not punish me in Your anger, do not chastise me in Your fury.
6:3 Have mercy on me, O LORD, for I languish; heal me, O LORD, for my bones shake with terror.
6:4 My whole soul is shaken with terror; and You, O LORD—how long?
6:5 Turn, O LORD, rescue me; deliver me as befits Your faithfulness.
6:6 For there is no praise of You among the dead; in Sheol, who can acclaim You?
6:7 I am weary with groaning; every night I drench my bed, I melt my couch with my tears.
6:8 My eyes are wasted with vexation, worn out because of all my foes.
6:9 Depart from me, all you evildoers, for the LORD heeds the sound of my weeping.
6:10 The LORD heeds my plea; the LORD accepts my prayer.
6:11 All my enemies will be frustrated and stricken with terror; they will turn back in an instant, confounded.

text.NRSV:
7:1 O Lord my God, in you I take refuge; save me from all my pursuers, and deliver me,
7:2 or like a lion they will tear me apart; they will drag me away, with no one to rescue.
7:3 O Lord my God, if I have done this, if there is wrong in my hands,
7:4 if I have repaid my ally with harm or plundered my foe without cause,
7:5 then let the enemy pursue and overtake me, trample my life to the ground, and lay my soul in the dust. Selah
7:6 Rise up, O Lord, in your anger; lift yourself up against the fury of my enemies; awake, O my God; you have appointed a judgment.
7:7 Let the assembly of the peoples be gathered around you, and over it take your seat on high.
7:8 The Lord judges the peoples; judge me, O Lord, according to my righteousness and according to the integrity that is in me.
7:9 O let the evil of the wicked come to an end, but establish the righteous, you who test the minds and hearts, O righteous God.
7:10 God is my shield, who saves the upright in heart.
7:11 God is a righteous judge, and a God who has indignation every day.
7:12 If one does not repent, God will whet his sword; he has bent and strung his bow;
7:13 he has prepared his deadly weapons, making his arrows fiery shafts.
7:14 See how they conceive evil, and are pregnant with mischief, and bring forth lies.
7:15 They make a pit, digging it out, and fall into the hole that they have made.
7:16 Their mischief returns upon their own heads, and on their own heads their violence descends.
7:17 I will give to the Lord the thanks due to his righteousness, and sing praise to the name of the Lord, the Most High.
text.Coverdale:
7:1 O LORD my God, I take refuge in you; *
      save and deliver me from all who pursue me;
7:2 Lest like a lion they tear me in pieces *
      and snatch me away with none to deliver me.
7:3 O LORD my God, if I have done these things: *
      if there is any wickedness in my hands,
7:4 If I have repaid my friend with evil, *
      or plundered him who without cause is my enemy;
7:5 Then let my enemy pursue and overtake me, *
      trample my life into the ground,
      and lay my honor in the dust.
7:6 Stand up, O LORD, in your wrath; *
      rise up against the fury of my enemies.
7:7 Awake, O my God, decree justice; *
      let the assembly of the peoples gather round you.
7:8 Be seated on your lofty throne, O Most High; *
      O LORD, judge the nations.
7:9 Give judgment for me according to my righteousness, O LORD, *
      and according to my innocence, O Most High.
7:10 Let the malice of the wicked come to an end,
      but establish the righteous; *
      for you test the mind and heart, O righteous God.
7:11 God is my shield and defense; *
      he is the savior of the true in heart.
7:12 God is a righteous judge; *
      God sits in judgment every day.
7:13 If they will not repent, God will whet his sword; *
      he will bow his bow and make it ready.
7:14 He has prepared his weapons of death; *
      he makes his arrows shafts of fire.
7:15 Look at those who are in labor with wickedness, *
      who conceive evil, and give birth to a lie.
7:16 They dig a pit and make it deep *
      and fall into the hole that they have made.
7:17 Their malice turns back upon their own head; *
      their violence falls on their own scalp.
7:18 I will bear witness that the LORD is righteous; *
      I will praise the Name of the LORD Most High.
text.KJV:
7:1 O LORD my God, in thee do I put my trust: save me from all them that persecute me, and deliver me:
7:2 Lest he tear my soul like a lion, rending it in pieces, while there is none to deliver.
7:3 O LORD my God, if I have done this; if there be iniquity in my hands;
7:4 If I have rewarded evil unto him that was at peace with me; (yea, I have delivered him that without cause is mine enemy:)
7:5 Let the enemy persecute my soul, and take it; yea, let him tread down my life upon the earth, and lay mine honour in the dust. Selah.
7:6 Arise, O LORD, in thine anger, lift up thyself because of the rage of mine enemies: and awake for me to the judgment that thou hast commanded.
7:7 So shall the congregation of the people compass thee about: for their sakes therefore return thou on high.
7:8 The LORD shall judge the people: judge me, O LORD, according to my righteousness, and according to mine integrity that is in me.
7:9 Oh let the wickedness of the wicked come to an end; but establish the just: for the righteous God trieth the hearts and reins.
7:10 My defence is of God, which saveth the upright in heart.
7:11 God judgeth the righteous, and God is angry with the wicked every day.
7:12 If he turn not, he will whet his sword; he hath bent his bow, and made it ready.
7:13 He hath also prepared for him the instruments of death; he ordaineth his arrows against the persecutors.
7:14 Behold, he travaileth with iniquity, and hath conceived mischief, and brought forth falsehood.
7:15 He made a pit, and digged it, and is fallen into the ditch which he made.
7:16 His mischief shall return upon his own head, and his violent dealing shall come down upon his own pate.
7:17 I will praise the LORD according to his righteousness: and will sing praise to the name of the LORD most high.
text.JPS:
7:1 A shiggaion of David, which he sang to the LORD concerning Cush, a Benjaminite.
7:2 O LORD, my God, in You I take refuge; deliver me from all my pursuers and save me;
7:3 lest they tear me like a lion, rending me in pieces, with no one to deliver.
7:4 O LORD, my God, if I have done this, if there is injustice on my hands,
7:5 if I have requited my ally with evil, or completely despoiled my enemy,
7:6 let the enemy pursue and overtake me; let him trample my life to the ground, and lay my honor in the dust. Selah.
7:7 Rise, O LORD, in Your anger; assert Yourself against the fury of my foes; bestir Yourself on my behalf; You have ordained judgment.
7:8 Let the assembly of peoples gather about You; sit enthroned on high above them.
7:9 The LORD judges the peoples; vindicate me, O LORD, according to my righteousness and my integrity.
7:10 Let the evil of the wicked come to an end, but establish the righteous; He who probes the mind and conscience is a righteous God.
7:11 My shield is with God, who delivers the upright in heart.
7:12 God is a righteous judge, and God pronounces doom each day.
7:13 If one does not turn back, He whets His sword, bends His bow and aims it;
7:14 He prepares His deadly weapons, sharpens His flashing arrows.
7:15 See, he hatches evil, conceives mischief, and gives birth to lies.
7:16 He digs a pit and deepens it, and falls into the trap he made.
7:17 His mischief recoils upon his own head; his lawlessness comes down upon his skull.
7:18 I will praise the LORD for His righteousness, and sing a hymn to the name of the LORD Most High.

text.NRSV:
8:1 O Lord, our Sovereign, how majestic is your name in all the earth! You have set your glory above the heavens.
8:2 Out of the mouths of babes and infants you have founded a bulwark because of your foes, to silence the enemy and the avenger.
8:3 When I look at your heavens, the work of your fingers, the moon and the stars that you have established;
8:4 what are human beings that you are mindful of them, mortals that you care for them?
8:5 Yet you have made them a little lower than God, and crowned them with glory and honor.
8:6 You have given them dominion over the works of your hands; you have put all things under their feet,
8:7 all sheep and oxen, and also the beasts of the field,
8:8 the birds of the air, and the fish of the sea, whatever passes along the paths of the seas.
8:9 O Lord, our Sovereign, how majestic is your name in all the earth!
text.Coverdale:
8:1 O LORD our Governor, *
      how exalted is your Name in all the world!
8:2 Out of the mouths of infants and children *
      your majesty is praised above the heavens.
8:3 You have set up a stronghold against your adversaries, *
      to quell the enemy and the avenger.
8:4 When I consider your heavens, the work of your fingers, *
      the moon and the stars you have set in their courses,
8:5 What is man that you should be mindful of him? *
      the son of man that you should seek him out?
8:6 You have made him but little lower than the angels; *
      you adorn him with glory and honor;
8:7 You give him mastery over the works of your hands; *
      you put all things under his feet:
8:8 All sheep and oxen, *
      even the wild beasts of the field,
8:9 The birds of the air, the fish of the sea, *
      and whatsoever walks in the paths of the sea.
8:10 O LORD our Governor, *
      how exalted is your Name in all the world!
text.KJV:
8:1 O LORD our Lord, how excellent is thy name in all the earth! who hast set thy glory above the heavens.
8:2 Out of the mouth of babes and sucklings hast thou ordained strength because of thine enemies, that thou mightest still the enemy and the avenger.
8:3 When I consider thy heavens, the work of thy fingers, the moon and the stars, which thou hast ordained;
8:4 What is man, that thou art mindful of him? and the son of man, that thou visitest him?
8:5 For thou hast made him a little lower than the angels, and hast crowned him with glory and honour.
8:6 Thou madest him to have dominion over the works of thy hands; thou hast put all things under his feet:
8:7 All sheep and oxen, yea, and the beasts of the field;
8:8 The fowl of the air, and the fish of the sea, and whatsoever passeth through the paths of the seas.
8:9 O LORD our Lord, how excellent is thy name in all the earth!
text.JPS:
8:1 For the leader; on the gittith. A psalm of David.
8:2 O LORD, our Lord, how majestic is Your name throughout the earth, You who have covered the heavens with Your splendor!
8:3 From the mouths of infants and sucklings You have founded strength on account of Your foes, to put an end to foe and avenger.
8:4 When I behold Your heavens, the work of Your fingers, the moon and stars that You set in place,
8:5 what is man that You have been mindful of him, mortal man that You have taken note of him,
8:6 that You have made him little less than divine, and adorned him with glory and majesty;
8:7 You have made him master over Your handiwork, laying the world at his feet,
8:8 all sheep and oxen, all the beasts of the field,
8:9 the birds of the sky, the fish of the sea, whatever travels the paths of the seas.
8:10 O LORD, our Lord, how majestic is Your name throughout the earth!

text.NRSV:
9:1 I will give thanks to you, O Lord, with my whole heart; I will tell of all your marvelous works.
9:2 I will be glad and exult in you; I will sing psalms to your Name, O Most High.
9:3 When my enemies are driven back, they will stumble and perish at your presence.
9:4 For you have maintained my right and my cause; you are seated on your throne judging righteously.
9:5 You have rebuked the ungodly and destroyed the wicked; you have blotted out their name for ever and ever.
9:6 As for the enemy, they are finished, in perpetual ruin, their cities plowed under, the very memory of them has perished.
9:7 But the Lord enthroned for ever; he has set up his throne for judgment.
9:8 It is he who rules the world with righteousness; he judges the peoples with equity.
9:9 The Lord will be a refuge for the oppressed, a refuge in time of trouble.
9:10 Those who know your Name will put their trust in you, for you never forsake those who seek you, O Lord.
9:11 Sing psalms to the Lord who dwells in Zion; proclaim among the peoples the things he has done.
9:12 The Avenger of blood will remember them; he will not forget the cry of the afflicted.
9:13 Have pity on me, O Lord; see the misery I suffer from those who hate me, O you who lift me up from the gates of death;
9:14 So that I may tell of all your praises and rejoice in your salvation in the gates of the city of Zion.
9:15 The ungodly have fallen into the pit they dug, and in the snare they set is their own foot caught.
9:16 The Lord is known by his acts of justice; the wicked are trapped in the works of their own hands.
9:17 The wicked shall be given over to the grave, and also all the peoples that forget God.
9:18 For the needy shall not always be forgotten, and the hope of the poor shall not perish for ever.
9:19 Rise up, O Lord, let not the ungodly have the upper hand; let them be judged before you.
9:20 Put them in fear, O Lord; let the ungodly know they are but mortal.

text.KJV:
9:1 I will praise thee, O LORD, with my whole heart; I will shew forth all thy marvellous works.
9:2 I will be glad and rejoice in thee: I will sing praise to thy name, O thou most High.
9:3 When mine enemies are turned back, they shall fall and perish at thy presence.
9:4 For thou hast maintained my right and my cause; thou satest in the throne judging right.
9:5 Thou hast rebuked the heathen, thou hast destroyed the wicked, thou hast put out their name for ever and ever.
9:6 O thou enemy, destructions are come to a perpetual end: and thou hast destroyed cities; their memorial is perished with them.
9:7 But the LORD shall endure for ever: he hath prepared his throne for judgment.
9:8 And he shall judge the world in righteousness, he shall minister judgment to the people in uprightness.
9:9 The LORD also will be a refuge for the oppressed, a refuge in times of trouble.
9:10 And they that know thy name will put their trust in thee: for thou, LORD, hast not forsaken them that seek thee.
9:11 Sing praises to the LORD, which dwelleth in Zion: declare among the people his doings.
9:12 When he maketh inquisition for blood, he remembereth them: he forgetteth not the cry of the humble.
9:13 Have mercy upon me, O LORD; consider my trouble which I suffer of them that hate me, thou that liftest me up from the gates of death:
9:14 That I may shew forth all thy praise in the gates of the daughter of Zion: I will rejoice in thy salvation.
9:15 The heathen are sunk down in the pit that they made: in the net which they hid is their own foot taken.
9:16 The LORD is known by the judgment which he executeth: the wicked is snared in the work of his own hands. Higgaion. Selah.
9:17 The wicked shall be turned into hell, and all the nations that forget God.
9:18 For the needy shall not alway be forgotten: the expectation of the poor shall not perish for ever.
9:19 Arise, O LORD; let not man prevail: let the heathen be judged in thy sight.
9:20 Put them in fear, O LORD: that the nations may know themselves to be but men. Selah.
text.JPS:
9:1 For the leader; almuth labben. A psalm of David.
9:2 I will praise the LORD with all my heart; I will tell of all Your wondrous deeds.
9:3 I will share Your joy and exultation; I will sing a hymn to Your name, O Most High.
9:4 When my enemies turn back, they stumble and perish before You;
9:5 for You upheld my right and my claim, sat on the throne as righteous judge.
9:6 You blasted nations, destroyed the wicked, blotted out their name forever and ever.
9:7 The enemy is ruined, cut off forever; You uprooted their cities, their very names are forgotten.
9:8 But the LORD is enthroned forever; He has set up His throne for judgment.
9:9 He judges the world with righteousness; He rules the peoples with equity.
9:10 The LORD is a haven for the oppressed, a haven in times of distress.
9:11 Those who know Your name trust You, for You do not abandon those who turn to You, O LORD.
9:12 Sing a hymn to the LORD, who dwells in Zion; declare His deeds among the peoples.
9:13 For He who avenges blood remembers them; He does not forget the cry of the afflicted.
9:14 Have mercy on me, O LORD; see my affliction at the hands of my foes, You who lift me up from the gates of death,
9:15 so that I may tell all Your praises in the gates of the Daughter of Zion, and rejoice in Your deliverance.
9:16 The nations sink into the pit they made; their own foot is caught in the net they hid.
9:17 The LORD has made Himself known and executed judgment; the wicked are ensnared by the work of their own hands. Higgaion. Selah.
9:18 The wicked shall depart to Sheol, all the nations that forget God.
9:19 For the needy shall not be ignored forever, nor the hope of the afflicted perish for all time.
9:20 Rise, O LORD! Let not man assert his power; let the nations be judged in Your presence.
9:21 Strike fear into them, O LORD; let the nations know they are but mortal. Selah.

text.NRSV:
10:1 Why do you stand so far off, O Lord, and hide yourself in time of trouble?
10:2 The wicked are so proud that they persecute the poor; let them be caught in the schemes they have devised.
10:3 For the wicked boast of their heart's desire and prosper; they curse the Lord and spurn him.
10:4 The wicked are so proud that they care not for God; their only thought is, 'There is no God.'
10:5 Their ways are prosperous at all times; your judgments are on high out of their sight; they scoff at all their enemies.
10:6 They say in their heart, 'I shall not be shaken; from age to age I shall not come to misfortune.'
10:7 Their mouth is full of cursing, deceit, and fraud; under their tongue are mischief and wrong.
10:8 They lurk in abandoned places and in secret places they murder the innocent; their eyes haunt the needy.
10:9 They lie in wait like a lion in a covert; they lie in wait to catch the helpless; they catch the helpless when they draw them into their net.
10:10 The helpless are crushed and sink down; they fall into the power of their wretchedness.
10:11 They say in their heart, 'God has forgotten; he hides his face; he will never see it.'
10:12 Rise up, O Lord; O God, lift up your hand; do not forget the afflicted.
10:13 Why should the wicked spurn God? Why should they say in their heart, 'You will not call to account'?
10:14 Surely you behold trouble and misery; you see it and take it into your own hand.
10:15 The helpless commits himself to you, for you are the helper of the orphans.
10:16 Break the power of the wicked and evil man; search out his wickedness until you find none.
10:17 The Lord is King for ever and ever; the ungodly shall perish from his land.
10:18 The Lord will hear the desire of the humble; you will strengthen their heart and your ear shall hear;
10:19 To give justice to the orphan and oppressed, so that mere mortals may strike terror no more.
text.Coverdale:
10:1 Why do you stand so far off, O LORD, *
      and hide yourself in time of trouble?
10:2 The wicked arrogantly persecute the poor, *
      but they are trapped in the schemes they have devised.
10:3 The wicked boast of their heart's desire; *
      the covetous curse and revile the LORD.
10:4 The wicked are so proud that they care not for God; *
      their only thought is, "God does not matter."
10:5 Their ways are devious at all times;
      your judgments are far above out of their sight; *
      they defy all their enemies.
10:6 They say in their heart, "I shall not be shaken; *
      no harm shall happen to me ever."
10:7 Their mouth is full of cursing, deceit, and oppression; *
      under their tongue are mischief and wrong.
10:8 They lurk in ambush in public squares
      and in secret places they murder the innocent; *
      they spy out the helpless.
10:9 They lie in wait, like a lion in a covert;
      they lie in wait to seize upon the lowly; *
      they seize the lowly and drag them away in their net.
10:10 The innocent are broken and humbled before them; *
      the helpless fall before their power.
10:11 They say in their heart, "God has forgotten; *
      he hides his face; he will never notice."
10:12 Rise up, O LORD;
      lift up your hand, O God; *
      do not forget the afflicted.
10:13 Why should the wicked revile God? *
      why should they say in their heart, "You do not care"?
10:14 Surely, you behold trouble and misery; *
      you see it and take it into your own hand.
10:15 The helpless commit themselves to you, *
      for you are the helper of orphans.
10:16 Break the power of the wicked and evil; *
      search out their wickedness until you find none.
10:17 The LORD is King for ever and ever; *
      the ungodly shall perish from his land.
10:18 The LORD will hear the desire of the humble; *
      you will strengthen their heart and your ears shall hear;
10:19 To give justice to the orphan and oppressed, *
      so that mere mortals may strike terror no more.
text.KJV:
10:1 Why standest thou afar off, O LORD? why hidest thou thyself in times of trouble?
10:2 The wicked in his pride doth persecute the poor: let them be taken in the devices that they have imagined.
10:3 For the wicked boasteth of his heart's desire, and blessess the covetous, whom the LORD abhorreth.
10:4 The wicked, through the pride of his countenance, will not seek after God: God is not in all his thoughts.
10:5 His ways are always grievous; thy judgments are far above out of his sight: as for all his enemies, he puffeth at them.
10:6 He hath said in his heart, I shall not be moved: for I shall never be in adversity.
10:7 His mouth is full of cursing and deceit and fraud: under his tongue is mischief and vanity.
10:8 He sitteth in the lurking places of the villages: in the secret places doth he murder the innocent: his eyes are privily set against the poor.
10:9 He lieth in wait secretly as a lion in his den: he lieth in wait to catch the poor: he doth catch the poor, when he draweth him into his net.
10:10 He croucheth, and humbleth himself, that the poor may fall by his strong ones.
10:11 He hath said in his heart, God hath forgotten: he hideth his face; he will never see it.
10:12 Arise, O LORD; O God, lift up thine hand: forget not the humble.
10:13 Wherefore doth the wicked contemn God? he hath said in his heart, Thou wilt not require it.
10:14 Thou hast seen it; for thou beholdest mischief and spite, to requite it with thy hand: the poor committeth himself unto thee; thou art the helper of the fatherless.
10:15 Break thou the arm of the wicked and the evil man: seek out his wickedness till thou find none.
10:16 The LORD is King for ever and ever: the heathen are perished out of his land.
10:17 LORD, thou hast heard the desire of the humble: thou wilt prepare their heart, thou wilt cause thine ear to hear:
10:18 To judge the fatherless and the oppressed, that the man of the earth may no more oppress.
text.JPS:
10:1 Why, O LORD, do You stand aloof, heedless in times of distress?
10:2 The wicked in his arrogance hounds the lowly—may they be caught in the schemes they devise!
10:3 The wicked boasts of his unbridled lusts; the grasping man reviles and scorns the LORD.
10:4 The wicked, arrogant, does not inquire; "There is no God" is his every thought.
10:5 His ways prosper at all times; Your judgments are far beyond his sight; he snorts at all his foes.
10:6 He says in his heart, "I shall not be shaken, through all generations I shall be free of trouble."
10:7 His mouth is full of oaths, deceit, and fraud; mischief and evil are under his tongue.
10:8 He lurks in ambush in the villages; in hiding places he murders the innocent; his eyes spy out the hapless.
10:9 He waits in a hiding place like a lion in his lair; he waits to seize the lowly; he seizes the lowly as he pulls his net taut.
10:10 He stoops, he crouches, and the hapless fall into his power.
10:11 He says in his heart, "God has forgotten; He hides His face; He never sees."
10:12 Rise, O LORD! Strike them down, O God! Do not forget the afflicted.
10:13 Why should the wicked scorn God, thinking in his heart, "You will not inquire"?
10:14 You do see! You take note of mischief and vexation, to requite them with Your hand. To You the hapless commits himself; You have ever been the helper of the orphan.
10:15 Break the arm of the wicked and evil man; trace his wickedness until You find no more of it.
10:16 The LORD is king forever and ever; the nations perish from His land.
10:17 You hear the desire of the meek, O LORD; You strengthen their heart; You incline Your ear,
10:18 to vindicate the orphan and the downtrodden, that mere man on earth may strike terror no more.

text.NRSV:
11:1 In the Lord have I taken refuge; how then can you say to me, 'Fly like a bird to the hilltop;
11:2 For see, the wicked bend the bow and fit the arrow to the string, to shoot from ambush at the true of heart.
11:3 When the foundations are being destroyed, what can the righteous do?'
11:4 The Lord is in his holy temple; the Lord's throne is in heaven.
11:5 His eyes behold the inhabited world; his gazing examines humankind.
11:6 The Lord examines the righteous and the wicked; he abhors those who love violence.
11:7 He shall rain coals of fire and brimstone upon the wicked; a scorching wind shall be their lot to drink.
11:8 For the Lord is righteous; he delights in righteous deeds; and the upright shall see his face.
text.Coverdale:
11:1 In the LORD have I taken refuge; *
      how then can you say to me,
      "Fly away like a bird to the hilltop;
11:2 For see how the wicked bend the bow
      and fit their arrows to the string, *
      to shoot from ambush at the true of heart.
11:3 When the foundations are being destroyed, *
      what can the righteous do?"
11:4 The LORD is in his holy temple; *
      the LORD's throne is in heaven.
11:5 His eyes behold the inhabited world; *
      his piercing eye weighs our worth.
11:6 The LORD weighs the righteous as well as the wicked, *
      but those who delight in violence he abhors.
11:7 Upon the wicked he shall rain coals of fire and burning sulphur; *
      a scorching wind shall be their lot.
11:8 For the LORD is righteous;
      he delights in righteous deeds; *
      and the just shall see his face.
text.KJV:
11:1 In the LORD put I my trust: how say ye to my soul, Flee as a bird to your mountain?
11:2 For, lo, the wicked bend their bow, they make ready their arrow upon the string, that they may privily shoot at the upright in heart.
11:3 If the foundations be destroyed, what can the righteous do?
11:4 The LORD is in his holy temple, the LORD'S throne is in heaven: his eyes behold, his eyelids try, the children of men.
11:5 The LORD trieth the righteous: but the wicked and him that loveth violence his soul hateth.
11:6 Upon the wicked he shall rain snares, fire and brimstone, and an horrible tempest: this shall be the portion of their cup.
11:7 For the righteous LORD loveth righteousness; his countenance doth behold the upright.
text.JPS:
11:1 For the leader. Of David. In the LORD I take refuge; how can you say to me, "Take to the hills like a bird!
11:2 For see, the wicked bend the bow, they set their arrow on the string to shoot from the shadows at the upright.
11:3 When the foundations are destroyed, what can the righteous man do?"
11:4 The LORD is in His holy palace; the LORD—His throne is in heaven. His eyes behold, His gaze examines mankind.
11:5 The LORD seeks out the righteous man, but He detests the wicked and the lover of violence.
11:6 He rains down upon the wicked blazing coals and sulfur; a scorching wind is their allotted cup.
11:7 For the LORD is righteous; He loves righteous deeds; the upright shall behold His face.

text.NRSV:
12:1 Help me, Lord, for there is no godly one left; the faithful have vanished from among us.
12:2 Everyone speaks falsely with his neighbor; with a smooth tongue they speak from a double heart.
12:3 Oh, that the Lord would cut off all smooth tongues, and close the lips that utter proud boasts!
12:4 Those who say, 'With our tongue will we prevail; our lips are our own: who is lord over us?'
12:5 'Because the needy are oppressed, and the poor cry out in misery, I will rise up,' says the Lord, 'and give them the help they long for.'
12:6 The words of the Lord are pure words, like silver refined from ore and purified seven times in the fire.
12:7 O Lord, protect us from this generation for ever.
12:8 The wicked prowl on every side, and that which is worthless is highly prized by everyone.
text.Coverdale:
12:1 Help me, LORD, for there is no godly one left; *
      the faithful have vanished from among us.
12:2 Everyone speaks falsely with his neighbor; *
      with a smooth tongue they speak from a double heart.
12:3 Oh, that the LORD would cut off all smooth tongues, *
      and close the lips that utter proud boasts!
12:4 Those who say, "With our tongue will we prevail; *
      our lips are our own; who is lord over us?"
12:5 "Because the needy are oppressed,
      and the poor cry out in misery, *
      I will rise up," says the LORD,
      "and give them the help they long for."
12:6 The words of the LORD are pure words, *
      like silver refined from ore
      and purified seven times in the fire.
12:7 O LORD, watch over us *
      and save us from this generation for ever.
12:8 The wicked prowl on every side, *
      and that which is worthless is highly prized by everyone.
text.KJV:
12:1 Help, LORD; for the godly man ceaseth; for the faithful fail from among the children of men.
12:2 They speak vanity every one with his neighbour: with flattering lips and with a double heart do they speak.
12:3 The LORD shall cut off all flattering lips, and the tongue that speaketh proud things:
12:4 Who have said, With our tongue will we prevail; our lips are our own: who is lord over us?
12:5 For the oppression of the poor, for the sighing of the needy, now will I arise, saith the LORD; I will set him in safety from him that puffeth at him.
12:6 The words of the LORD are pure words: as silver tried in a furnace of earth, purified seven times.
12:7 Thou shalt keep them, O LORD, thou shalt preserve them from this generation for ever.
12:8 The wicked walk on every side, when the vilest men are exalted.
text.JPS:
12:1 For the leader; on the sheminith. A psalm of David.
12:2 Help, O LORD! For the faithful are no more; the loyal have vanished from among men.
12:3 One speaks falsely to another; with flattering lips they talk, with duplicity of heart.
12:4 May the LORD cut off all flattering lips, every tongue that speaks arrogance,
12:5 those who say, "Our tongues will make us powerful; our lips are with us, who is our master?"
12:6 "Because of the plunder of the poor and the groan of the needy, I will now act," says the LORD; "I will grant safety to the one who pants for it."
12:7 The words of the LORD are pure words, silver purged in an earthen crucible, refined sevenfold.
12:8 You, O LORD, will keep them, guarding each from this generation forever.
12:9 On every side the wicked roam when baseness is exalted among men.

text.NRSV:
13:1 How long, O Lord? will you forget me for ever? how long will you hide your face from me?
13:2 How long shall I have perplexity in my mind, and grief in my heart, day after day? how long shall my enemy triumph over me?
13:3 Look upon me and answer me, O Lord my God; give light to my eyes, lest I sleep in death;
13:4 Lest my enemy say, 'I have prevailed over him,' and my foes rejoice that I have fallen.
13:5 But I put my trust in your mercy; my heart is joyful because of your saving help.
13:6 I will sing to the Lord, for he has dealt with me richly; I will praise the Name of the Lord Most High.
text.Coverdale:
13:1 How long, O LORD?
      will you forget me for ever? *
      how long will you hide your face from me?
13:2 How long shall I have perplexity in my mind,
      and grief in my heart, day after after day? *
      how long shall my enemy triumph over me?
13:3 Look upon me and answer me, O LORD my God; *
      give light to my eyes, lest I sleep in death;
13:4 Lest my enemy say, "I have prevailed over him," *
      and my foes rejoice that I have fallen.
13:5 But I put my trust in your mercy; *
      my heart is joyful because of your saving help.
13:6 I will sing to the LORD, for he has dealt with me richly; *
      I will praise the Name of the LORD Most High.
text.KJV:
13:1 How long wilt thou forget me, O LORD? for ever? how long wilt thou hide thy face from me?
13:2 How long shall I take counsel in my soul, having sorrow in my heart daily? how long shall mine enemy be exalted over me?
13:3 Consider and hear me, O LORD my God: lighten mine eyes, lest I sleep the sleep of death;
13:4 Lest mine enemy say, I have prevailed against him; and those that trouble me rejoice when I am moved.
13:5 But I have trusted in thy mercy; my heart shall rejoice in thy salvation.
13:6 I will sing unto the LORD, because he hath dealt bountifully with me.
text.JPS:
13:1 For the leader. A psalm of David.
13:2 How long, O LORD; will You ignore me forever? How long will You hide Your face from me?
13:3 How long must I harbor grief in my soul, sorrow in my heart by day? How long will my enemy have the upper hand?
13:4 Look at me, answer me, O LORD, my God! Restore the luster to my eyes, lest I sleep the sleep of death;
13:5 lest my enemy say, "I have overcome him," my foes exult when I totter.
13:6 But I trust in Your faithfulness; my heart will exult in Your deliverance. I will sing to the LORD, for He has been good to me.

text.NRSV:
14:1 The fool has said in his heart, 'There is no God.' All are corrupt and commit abominable acts; there is none who does any good.
14:2 The Lord looks down from heaven upon us all, to see if there is any who is wise, if there is one who seeks after God.
14:3 Everyone has proved faithless; all alike have turned bad; there is none who does good; no, not one.
14:4 Have they no knowledge, all those evildoers who eat up my people like bread and do not call upon the Lord?
14:5 See how they tremble with fear, because God is in the company of the righteous.
14:6 Their aim is to confound the plans of the afflicted, but the Lord is their refuge.
14:7 Oh, that Israel's deliverance would come out of Zion! when the Lord restores the fortunes of his people, Jacob will rejoice and Israel be glad.

text.KJV:
14:1 The fool hath said in his heart, There is no God. They are corrupt, they have done abominable works, there is none that doeth good.
14:2 The LORD looked down from heaven upon the children of men, to see if there were any that did understand, and seek God.
14:3 They are all gone aside, they are all together become filthy: there is none that doeth good, no, not one.
14:4 Have all the workers of iniquity no knowledge? who eat up my people as they eat bread, and call not upon the LORD.
14:5 There were they in great fear: for God is in the generation of the righteous.
14:6 Ye have shamed the counsel of the poor, because the LORD is his refuge.
14:7 Oh that the salvation of Israel were come out of Zion! when the LORD bringeth back the captivity of his people, Jacob shall rejoice, and Israel shall be glad.
text.JPS:
14:1 For the leader. Of David. The fool is convinced there is no God. They are corrupt, their deeds are vile; not one does good.
14:2 The LORD looks down from heaven upon mankind to see if any are wise, if any seek God.
14:3 All have turned turned away, all alike are tainted; not one does good, not even one.
14:4 Are they so ignorant, all the evildoers, who devour my people as they devour bread, and do not invoke the LORD?
14:5 There they will be seized with terror, for God is with the righteous generation.
14:6 You would flout the plans of the poor—but the LORD is his refuge.
14:7 O that the deliverance of Israel might come from Zion! When the LORD restores the fortunes of His people, Jacob will exult, Israel will rejoice.

text.NRSV:
15:1 Lord, who may dwell in your tabernacle? Who may abide upon your holy hill?
15:2 Whoever leads a blameless life and does what is right, who speaks the truth from his heart.
15:3 There is no guile upon his tongue; he does no evil to his friend; he does not heap contempt upon his neighbor.
15:4 In his sight the wicked is rejected, but he honors those who fear the Lord.
15:5 He has sworn to do no wrong and does not take back his word.
15:6 He does not give his money in hope of gain, nor does he take a bribe against the innocent.
15:7 Whoever does these things shall never be overthrown.

text.KJV:
15:1 LORD, who shall abide in thy tabernacle? who shall dwell in thy holy hill?
15:2 He that walketh uprightly, and worketh righteousness, and speaketh the truth in his heart.
15:3 He that backbitet_ not with his tongue, nor doeth evil to his neighbour, nor taketh up a reproach against his neighbour.
15:4 In whose eyes a vile person is contemned; but he honoureth them that fear the LORD. He that sweareth to his own hurt, and changeth not.
15:5 He that putteth not out his money to usury, nor taketh reward against the innocent. He that doeth these things shall never be moved.

text.NRSV:
16:1 Protect me, O God, for I take refuge in you; I have said to the Lord, 'You are my Lord, my good above all other.'
16:2 All my delight is upon the godly who are in the land, upon those who are noble among the people.
16:3 But those who run after other gods shall have their troubles multiplied.
16:4 Their libations of blood I will not offer, nor take the names of their gods upon my lips.
16:5 O Lord, you are my portion and my cup; it is you who uphold my lot.
16:6 My boundaries enclose a pleasant land; indeed, I have a goodly heritage.
16:7 I will bless the Lord who gives me counsel; my heart teaches me, night after night.
16:8 I have set the Lord always before me; because he is at my right hand, I shall not fall.
16:9 My heart, therefore, is glad, and my spirit rejoices; my body also shall rest in hope.
16:10 For you will not abandon me to the grave, nor let your holy one see the Pit.
16:11 You will show me the path of life; in your presence there is fullness of joy, and in your right hand are pleasures for evermore.

text.KJV:
16:1 Preserve me, O God: for in thee do I put my trust.
16:2 O my soul, thou hast said unto the LORD, Thou art my Lord: my goodness extendeth not to thee;
16:3 But to the saints that are in the earth, and to the excellent, in whom is all my delight.
16:4 Their sorrows shall be multiplied that hasten after another god: their drink offerings of blood will I not offer, nor take up their names into my lips.
16:5 The LORD is the portion of mine inheritance and of my cup: thou maintainest my lot.
16:6 The lines are fallen unto me in pleasant places; yea, I have a goodly heritage.
16:7 I will bless the LORD, who hath given me counsel: my reins also instruct me in the night seasons.
16:8 I have set the LORD always before me: because he is at my right hand, I shall not be moved.
16:9 Therefore my heart is glad, and my glory rejoiceth: my flesh also shall rest in hope.
16:10 For thou wilt not leave my soul in hell; neither wilt thou suffer thine Holy One to see corruption.
16:11 Thou wilt shew me the path of life: in thy presence is fulness of joy; at thy right hand there are pleasures for evermore.
text.JPS:
16:1 A michtam of David. Protect me, O God, for I seek refuge in You.
16:2 I say to the LORD, "You are my Lord; I have no good but You."
16:3 As to the holy and mighty ones that are in the land, my whole desire is for them.
16:4 Those who chase after another [god] shall multiply their sorrows; I will not pour out their libations of blood or bear their names on my lips.
16:5 LORD, You are my allotted portion and my cup; You guide my lot.
16:6 Delightful places have fallen to my lot; a lovely heritage is mine.
16:7 I bless the LORD who has guided me; my conscience admonishes me at night.
16:8 I am ever mindful of the LORD'S presence; He is at my right hand; I shall not be shaken.
16:9 So my heart rejoices, my whole being exults, and my body rests secure.
16:10 For You will not abandon me to Sheol, or let Your faithful one see the Pit.
16:11 You will teach me the path of life. In Your presence is perfect joy; delights are ever in Your right hand.

text.NRSV:
17:1 Hear my plea of innocence, O Lord; give heed to my cry; listen to my prayer, which does not come from lying lips.
17:2 Let my vindication come forth from your presence; let your eyes be fixed on justice.
17:3 Weigh my heart, summon me by night, melt me down; you will find no impurity in me; I give no offense with my mouth as others do; I have heeded the words of your lips.
17:4 My footsteps hold fast to the ways of your law; in your paths my feet shall not stumble.
17:5 I call upon you, O God, for you will answer me; incline your ear to me and hear my words.
17:6 Show me your marvelous loving-kindness, O Savior of those who take refuge at your right hand from those who rise up against them.
17:7 Keep me as the apple of your eye; hide me under the shadow of your wings,
17:8 From the wicked who assault me, from my deadly enemies who surround me.
17:9 They have closed their heart to pity, and their mouth speaks proud things.
17:10 They press me hard, now they surround me, watching how they may cast me to the ground,
17:11 Like a lion, greedy for its prey, and like a young lion lurking in secret places.
17:12 Rise up, O Lord; confront them and cast them down; deliver me from the wicked by your sword.
17:13 Deliver me, O Lord, by your hand from those whose portion in life is this world;
17:14 Whose bellies you fill with your treasure, who are sated with children and leave their wealth to their little ones.
17:15 As for me, I shall see your face in righteousness; when I awake and behold your likeness, I shall be satisfied.

text.KJV:
17:1 Hear the right, O LORD, attend unto my cry, give ear unto my prayer, that goeth not out of feigned lips.
17:2 Let my sentence come forth from thy presence; let thine eyes behold the things that are equal.
17:3 Thou hast proved mine heart; thou hast visited me in the night; thou hast tried me, and shalt find nothing; I am purposed that my mouth shall not transgress.
17:4 Concerning the works of men, by the word of thy lips I have kept me from the paths of the destroyer.
17:5 Hold up my goings in thy paths, that my footsteps slip not.
17:6 I have called upon thee, for thou wilt hear me, O God: incline thine ear unto me, and hear my speech.
17:7 Shew thy marvellous lovingkindness, O thou that savest by thy right hand them which put their trust in thee from those that rise up against them.
17:8 Keep me as the apple of the eye, hide me under the shadow of thy wings,
17:9 From the wicked that oppress me, from my deadly enemies, who compass me about.
17:10 They are inclosed in their own fat: with their mouth they speak proudly.
17:11 They have now compassed us in our steps: they have set their eyes bowing down to the earth;
17:12 Like as a lion that is greedy of his prey, and as it were a young lion lurking in secret places.
17:13 Arise, O LORD, disappoint him, cast him down: deliver my soul from the wicked, which is thy sword:
17:14 From men which are thy hand, O LORD, from men of the world, which have their portion in this life, and whose belly thou fillest with thy hid treasure: they are full of children, and leave the rest of their substance to their babes.
17:15 As for me, I will behold thy face in righteousness: I shall be satisfied, when I awake, with thy likeness.
text.JPS:
17:1 A prayer of David. Hear a just cause, O LORD, attend to my cry; give ear to my prayer from lips without deceit.
17:2 May my vindication come from You; may Your eyes behold what is right.
17:3 You have examined my heart, You have searched it by night; You have tried me and found nothing; I minded that my mouth should not offend.
17:4 As for man's dealings, in accordance with the command of Your lips, I have kept from the paths of the lawless.
17:5 My feet have held to Your tracks, my legs have not faltered.
17:6 I call upon You, for You will answer me, O God; turn Your ear to me, hear my speech.
17:7 Display Your wondrous faithfulness, O Deliverer of those who find refuge at Your right hand from their assailants.
17:8 Guard me as the apple of the eye; hide me in the shadow of Your wings
17:9 from the wicked who despoil me, from my mortal enemies who encircle me.
17:10 Their hearts are closed to pity; their mouths speak arrogance.
17:11 Now they crowd around us; they set their eyes to blaze a trail through the land.
17:12 He is like a lion eager for prey, a king of beasts lurking in hiding.
17:13 Rise, O LORD! Confront him! Bring him down! By Your sword rescue me from the wicked;
17:14 by Your hand, O LORD, from men—from men of the world whose portion is in this life, and whose bellies You fill with Your store; whose children are sated, and who leave their abundance to their babes.
17:15 Then I, justified, will behold Your face; awake, I am sated with a vision of You.

text.NRSV:
18:1 I love you, O Lord my strength,
18:2 O Lord my stronghold, my crag, and my haven; my God, my rock in whom I put my trust, my shield, the horn of my salvation, and my refuge; you are worthy of praise.
18:3 I will call upon the Lord, and so shall I be saved from my enemies.
18:4 The breakers of death rolled over me, and the torrents of oblivion made me afraid.
18:5 The cords of hell entangled me, and the snares of death were set for me.
18:6 I called upon the Lord in my distress and cried out to my God for help.
18:7 He heard my voice from his heavenly dwelling; my cry of anguish came to his ears.
18:8 The earth reeled and rocked; the roots of the mountains shook; they reeled because of his anger.
18:9 Smoke rose from his nostrils and a consuming fire out of his mouth; hot burning coals blazed forth from him.
18:10 He parted the heavens and came down with a storm cloud under his feet.
18:11 He rode upon a cherub and flew; he came swiftly on the wings of the wind.
18:12 He wrapped darkness about him; he made dark waters and thick clouds his pavilion.
18:13 From the brightness of his presence, through the clouds, burst hailstones and coals of fire.
18:14 The Lord thundered out of heaven; the Most High uttered his voice.
18:15 He loosed his arrows and scattered them; he hurled thunderbolts and routed them.
18:16 The beds of the seas were uncovered, and the foundations of the world laid bare, at your battle cry, O Lord, at the blast of the breath of your nostrils.
18:17 He reached down from on high and grasped me; he drew me out of great waters.
18:18 He delivered me from my strong enemies and from those who hated me; for they were too mighty for me.
18:19 They confronted me in the day of my disaster; but the Lord was my support.
18:20 He brought me out into an open place; he rescued me because he delighted in me.
18:21 The Lord rewarded me because of my righteous dealing; because my hands were clean he rewarded me;
18:22 For I have kept the ways of the Lord and have not offended against my God;
18:23 For all his judgments are before my eyes, and his decrees I have not put away from me;
18:24 For I have been blameless with him and have kept myself from iniquity;
18:25 Therefore the Lord rewarded me according to my righteous dealing, because of the cleanness of my hands in his sight.
18:26 With the faithful you show yourself faithful, O God; with the forthright you show yourself forthright.
18:27 With the pure you show yourself pure, but with the crooked you are wily.
18:28 You will save a lowly people, but you will humble the haughty eyes.
18:29 You, O Lord, are my lamp; my God, you make my darkness bright.
18:30 With you I will break down an enclosure; with the help of my God I will scale any wall.
18:31 As for God, his ways are perfect; the words of the Lord are tried in the fire; he is a shield to all who trust in him.
18:32 For who is God, but the Lord? who is the Rock, except our God?
18:33 It is God who girds me about with strength and makes my way secure.
18:34 He makes me sure-footed like a deer and lets me stand firm on the heights.
18:35 He trains my hands for battle, and my arms for bending even a bow of bronze.
18:36 You have given me your shield of victory; your right hand also sustains me; your loving care makes me great.
18:37 You lengthen my stride beneath me, and my ankles do not give way.
18:38 I pursue my enemies and overtake them; I will not turn back till I have destroyed them.
18:39 I strike them down, and they cannot rise; they fall defeated at my feet.
18:40 You have girded me with strength for the battle; you have cast down my adversaries beneath me; you have made my enemies turn their backs upon me, and I destroyed those who hated me.
18:41 They cried for help, but there was none to save them; they cried to the Lord, but he did not answer.
18:42 I beat them small like dust before the wind; I trampled them like mud in the streets.
18:43 You delivered me from strife with the peoples; you made me head over nations; people whom I had not known served me.
18:44 As soon as they heard of me, they obeyed me; foreigners came cringing to me.
18:45 Foreigners lost heart and came trembling out of their strongholds.
18:46 The Lord lives! Blessed is my rock! Exalted is the God of my salvation!
18:47 He is the God who gave me victory and cast down the peoples beneath me.
18:48 You rescued me from the fury of my enemies; you exalted me above those who rose against me; you saved me from my deadly foe.
18:49 Therefore will I extol you among the nations, O Lord, and sing praises to your Name.
18:50 He multiplies the victories of his king; he shows loving-kindness to his anointed, to David and his descendants for ever.

text.KJV:
18:1 I will love thee, O LORD, my strength.
18:2 The LORD is my rock, and my fortress, and my deliverer; my God, my strength, in whom I will trust; my buckler, and the horn of my salvation, and my high tower.
18:3 I will call upon the LORD, who is worthy to be praised: so shall I be saved from mine enemies.
18:4 The sorrows of death compassed me, and the floods of ungodly men made me afraid.
18:5 The sorrows of hell compassed me about: the snares of death prevented me.
18:6 In my distress I called upon the LORD, and cried unto my God: he heard my voice out of his temple, and my cry came before him, even into his ears.
18:7 Then the earth shook and trembled; the foundations also of the hills moved and were shaken, because he was wroth.
18:8 There went up a smoke out of his nostrils, and fire out of his mouth devoured: coals were kindled by it.
18:9 He bowed the heavens also, and came down: and darkness was under his feet.
18:10 And he rode upon a cherub, and did fly: yea, he did fly upon the wings of the wind.
18:11 He made darkness his secret place; his pavilion round about him were dark waters and thick clouds of the skies.
18:12 At the brightness that was before him his thick clouds passed, hail stones and coals of fire.
18:13 The LORD also thundered in the heavens, and the Highest gave his voice; hail stones and coals of fire.
18:14 Yea, he sent out his arrows, and scattered them; and he shot out lightnings, and discomfited them.
18:15 Then the channels of waters were seen, and the foundations of the world were discovered at thy rebuke, O LORD, at the blast of the breath of thy nostrils.
18:16 He sent from above, he took me, he drew me out of many waters.
18:17 He delivered me from my strong enemy, and from them which hated me: for they were too strong for me.
18:18 They prevented me in the day of my calamity: but the LORD was my stay.
18:19 He brought me forth also into a large place; he delivered me, because he delighted in PDF.
18:20 The LORD rewarded me according to my righteousness; according to the cleanness of my hands hath he recompensed me.
18:21 For I have kept the ways of the LORD, and have not wickedly departed from my God.
18:22 For all his judgments were before me, and I did not put away his statutes from me.
18:23 I was also upright before him, and I kept myself from mine iniquity.
18:24 Therefore hath the LORD recompensed me according to my righteousness, according to the cleanness of my hands in his eyesight.
18:25 With the merciful thou wilt shew thyself merciful; with an upright man thou wilt shew thyself upright;
18:26 With the pure thou wilt shew thyself pure; and with the froward thou wilt shew thyself froward.
18:27 For thou wilt save the afflicted people; but wilt bring down high looks.
18:28 For thou wilt light my candle: the LORD my God will enlighten my darkness.
18:29 For by thee I have run through a troop; and by my God have I leaped over a wall.
18:30 As for God, his way is perfect: the word of the LORD is tried: he is a buckler to all those that trust in him.
18:31 For who is God save the LORD? or who is a rock save our God?
18:32 It is God that girdeth me with strength, and maketh my way perfect.
18:33 He maketh my feet like hinds' feet, and setteth me upon my high places.
18:34 He teacheth my hands to war, so that a bow of steel is broken by mine arms.
18:35 Thou hast also given me the shield of thy salvation: and thy right hand hath holden me up, and thy gentleness hath made me great.
18:36 Thou hast enlarged my steps under me, that my feet did not slip.
18:37 I have pursued mine enemies, and overtaken them: neither did I turn again till they were consumed.
18:38 I have wounded them that they were not able to rise: they are fallen under my feet.
18:39 For thou hast girded me with strength unto the battle: thou hast subdued under me those that rose up against me.
18:40 Thou hast also given me the necks of mine enemies; that I might destroy them that hate me.
18:41 They cried, but there was none to save them: even unto the LORD, but he answered them not.
18:42 Then did I beat them small as the dust before the wind: I did cast them out as the dirt in the streets.
18:43 Thou hast delivered me from the strivings of the people; and thou hast made me the head of the heathen: a people whom I have not known shall serve me.
18:44 As soon as they hear of me, they shall obey me: the strangers shall submit themselves unto me.
18:45 The strangers shall fade away, and be afraid out of their close places.
18:46 The LORD liveth; and blessed be my rock; and exalted be the God of the salvation of my deliverance.
18:47 It is God that avengeth me, and subdueth the people under me.
18:48 He delivereth me from mine enemies: yea, thou liftest me up above those that rise up against me: thou hast delivered me from the violent man.
18:49 Therefore will I give thanks unto thee, O LORD, among the heathen, and sing praises unto thy name.
18:50 Great deliverance giveth he to his king; and sheweth mercy to his anointed, to David, and to his seed for evermore.
text.JPS:
18:1 For the leader. Of David, the servant of the LORD, who addressed the words of this song to the LORD on the day that the LORD delivered him from the hands of all his enemies and from the hand of Saul.
18:2 He said: I love You, O LORD, my strength.
18:3 The LORD is my rock, my fortress, and my deliverer; my God, my rock in whom I seek refuge; my shield, my mighty champion, my haven.
18:4 All praise! I called upon the LORD, and I was delivered from my enemies.
18:5 Ropes of Death encompassed me; torrents of Belial terrified me;
18:6 ropes of Sheol encircled me; snares of Death confronted me.
18:7 In my distress I called upon the LORD, cried out to my God; from His temple He heard my voice, my cry to Him reached His ears.
18:8 Then the earth rocked and quaked; the foundations of the mountains shook, they rocked because He was angry.
18:9 Smoke went up from His nostrils, from His mouth a devouring fire; live coals blazed forth from Him.
18:10 He bent the sky and came down, thick cloud beneath His feet.
18:11 He mounted a cherub and flew, gliding on the wings of the wind.
18:12 He made darkness His screen; dark thunderclouds, dense clouds of the sky, His pavilion round about Him.
18:13 Out of the brilliance before Him, hail and fiery coals broke through His clouds.
18:14 The LORD thundered from heaven, the Most High gave forth His voice—hail and fiery coals.
18:15 He let fly His arrows and scattered them; He discharged lightning flashes and routed them.
18:16 The ocean-bed was exposed; the foundations of the world were laid bare by Your rebuke, O LORD, by the blast of the breath of Your nostrils.
18:17 He reached down from on high, He took me; He drew me out of the mighty waters.
18:18 He rescued me from my fierce enemy, from foes too mighty for me.
18:19 They confronted me on the day of my calamity, but the LORD was my support.
18:20 He brought me out to a spacious place; He rescued me because He desired me.
18:21 The LORD rewarded me according to my merit; He requited me according to the cleanness of my hands.
18:22 For I have kept the ways of the LORD and have not wickedly departed from my God.
18:23 For all His rules are before me, and I do not scale code from His laws.
18:24 I have been blameless before Him, and have guarded myself from sinning.
18:25 The LORD requited me according to my merit, the cleanness of my hands in His sight.
18:26 With the faithful You deal faithfully; with the blameless man You deal blameless;
18:27 with the pure You deal purely, and with the perverse You are tortuous.
18:28 It is You who deliver lowly folk, but haughty eyes You abase.
18:29 It is You who light my lamp; the LORD, my God, lights up my darkness.
18:30 With You I can rush a barrier, with my God I can scale a wall.
18:31 The way of God is perfect; the word of the LORD is pure; He is a shield to all who seek refuge in Him.
18:32 For who is a god but the LORD? Who is a rock except our God?—
18:33 the God who girded me with might, who made my way perfect;
18:34 who made my legs like a deer's, and set me firm on the heights;
18:35 who trained my hands for battle, so that my arms can bend a bow of bronze.
18:36 You have given me Your shielding deliverance; Your right hand has sustained me, and Your care has made me great.
18:37 You have made my strides wide beneath me, and my feet have not faltered.
18:38 I pursued my enemies and overtook them; I did not turn back till they were destroyed.
18:39 I smashed them so they could not rise; they fell beneath my feet.
18:40 You girded me with might for battle, overthrew my assailants before me.
18:41 You made my enemies turn tail before me; I wiped out those who hated me.
18:42 They cried out, but there was none to deliver; to the LORD, but He did not answer them.
18:43 I ground them fine as wind-blown dust; I stamped them flat as dirt in the street.
18:44 You rescued me from the strifes of people; You kept me at the head of nations; a people I knew not served me.
18:45 At the mere report of me they are submissive; foreigners cower before me;
18:46 foreigners lose heart, and come trembling from their fastnesses.
18:47 The LORD lives! Blessed is my rock! Exalted be God, my Deliverer—
18:48 the God who vindicated me, who subjected peoples to me,
18:49 who rescued me from my enemies, who exalted me above my assailants, who delivered me from lawless men.
18:50 For this I praise You among the nations, O LORD, and sing a hymn to Your name,
18:51 a tower of deliverance to His king, who deals faithfully with His anointed, with David and his offspring forever.

text.NRSV:
19:1 The heavens declare the glory of God, and the firmament shows his handiwork.
19:2 One day tells its tale to another, and one night imparts knowledge to another.
19:3 Although they have no words or language, and their voices are not heard,
19:4 Their sound has gone out into all lands, and their message to the ends of the world.
19:5 In the deep has he set a pavilion for the sun; it comes forth like a bridegroom out of his chamber; it rejoices like a champion to run its course.
19:6 It goes forth from the uttermost edge of the heavens and runs about to the end of it again; nothing is hidden from its burning heat.
19:7 The law of the Lord is perfect and revives the soul; the testimony of the Lord is sure and gives wisdom to the innocent.
19:8 The statutes of the Lord are just and rejoice the heart; the commandment of the Lord is clear and gives light to the eyes.
19:9 The fear of the Lord is clean and endures for ever; the judgments of the Lord are true and righteous altogether.
19:10 More to be desired are they than gold, more than much fine gold, sweeter far than honey, than honey in the comb.
19:11 By them also is your servant enlightened, and in keeping them there is great reward.
19:12 Who can tell how often he offends? cleanse me from my secret faults.
19:13 Above all, keep your servant from presumptuous sins; let them not get dominion over me; then shall I be whole and sound, and innocent of a great offense.
19:14 Let the words of my mouth and the meditation of my heart be acceptable in your sight, O Lord, my strength and my redeemer.
