text.KJV:
34:1 I will bless the LORD at all times: his praise shall continually be in my mouth.
34:2 My soul shall make her boast in the LORD: the humble shall hear thereof, and be glad.
34:3 O magnify the LORD with me, and let us exalt his name together.
34:4 I sought the LORD, and he heard me, and delivered me from all my fears.
34:5 They looked unto him, and were lightened: and their faces were not ashamed.
34:6 This poor man cried, and the LORD heard him, and saved him out of all his troubles.
34:7 The angel of the LORD encampeth round about them that fear him, and delivereth them.
34:8 O taste and see that the LORD is good: blessed is the man that trusteth in him.
34:9 O fear the LORD, ye his saints: for there is no want to them that fear him.
34:10 The young lions do lack, and suffer hunger: but they that seek the LORD shall not want any good thing.
34:11 Come, ye children, hearken unto me: I will teach you the fear of the LORD.
34:12 What man is he that desireth life, and loveth many days, that he may see good?
34:13 Keep thy tongue from evil, and thy lips from speaking guile.
34:14 Depart from evil, and do good; seek peace, and pursue it.
34:15 The eyes of the LORD are upon the righteous, and his ears are open unto their cry.
34:16 The face of the LORD is against them that do evil, to cut off the remembrance of them from the earth.
34:17 The righteous cry, and the LORD heareth, and delivereth them out of all their troubles.
34:18 The LORD is nigh unto them that are of a broken heart; and saveth such as be of a contrite spirit.
34:19 Many are the afflictions of the righteous: but the LORD delivereth him out of them all.
34:20 He keepeth all his bones: not one of them is broken.
34:21 Evil shall slay the wicked: and they that hate the righteous shall be desolate.
34:22 The LORD redeemeth the soul of his servants: and none of them that trust in him shall be desolate.
text.JPS:
34:1 Of David, when he feigned madness before Abimelech, who drove him out, and he went away.
34:2 I will bless the LORD at all times; His praise is ever in my mouth.
34:3 My soul glories in the LORD; let the lowly hear it and rejoice.
34:4 Exalt the LORD with me; let us extol His name together.
34:5 I sought the LORD and He answered me, and delivered me from all my terrors.
34:6 Look to Him and be radiant; let your faces not be downcast.
34:7 Here was a lowly man who cried, and the LORD heard him and delivered him from all his troubles.
34:8 The angel of the LORD camps around those who fear Him and rescues them.
34:9 Taste and see that the LORD is good; happy is the man who takes refuge in Him.
34:10 Fear the LORD, you His holy ones, for those who fear Him lack nothing.
34:11 Lions may suffer want and go hungry, but those who seek the LORD lack no good thing.
34:12 Come, children, listen to me; I will teach you the fear of the LORD.
34:13 Who is the man who desires life, who loves days to see good things?
34:14 Keep your tongue from evil, your lips from speaking guile.
34:15 Shun evil and do good; seek amity and pursue it.
34:16 The eyes of the LORD are on the righteous, and His ears are open to their cry.
34:17 The face of the LORD is set against evildoers, to erase their memory from the earth.
34:18 They cry out, and the LORD hears, and delivers them from all their troubles.
34:19 The LORD is close to the brokenhearted, and delivers those whose spirit is crushed.
34:20 Many are the misfortunes of the righteous man, but the LORD delivers him from them all.
34:21 He keeps all his bones; not one of them is broken.
34:22 Misfortune kills the wicked; the enemies of the righteous shall be ruined.
34:23 The LORD redeems the life of His servants; all who take refuge in Him shall not be ruined.

text.NRSV:
35:1 Fight those who fight me, O Lord; attack those who are attacking me.
35:2 Take up shield and armor and rise up to help me.
35:3 Draw the sword and bar the way against those who pursue me; say to my soul, 'I am your salvation.'
35:4 Let those who seek my life be shamed and disgraced; let those who plot my ruin fall back and be dismayed.
35:5 Let them be like chaff before the wind, with the angel of the Lord driving them on.
35:6 Let their way be dark and slippery, with the angel of the Lord pursuing them.
35:7 For without cause they have laid a net in a pit for me; without cause they have dug a trap for my life.
35:8 Let ruin come upon them unawares; let them be caught in the net they laid; let them fall into their own pit of ruin.
35:9 Then will I be joyful in the Lord; I will glory in his victory.
35:10 My whole being will say, 'Lord, who is like you? You deliver the poor from those who are too strong for them, the poor and needy from those who rob them.'
35:11 Malicious witnesses rise up against me; they charge me with matters I know nothing about.
35:12 They repay me evil for good, to my intense sorrow.
35:13 As for me, when they were sick, I wore sackcloth and humbled myself by fasting; I prayed with my whole heart.
35:14 I walked about as for my friend or my brother; I went in mourning as one who laments his mother.
35:15 But when I stumbled, they were glad and gathered together; they gathered against me; strangers whom I did not know mocked me and did not cease.
35:16 Like profane mockers at a feast, they gnashed at me with their teeth.
35:17 O Lord, how long will you look on? rescue me from the roaring beasts, and my life from the young lions.
35:18 I will give you thanks in the great assembly; I will praise you in the mighty throng.
35:19 Do not let my treacherous enemies rejoice over me, nor let those who hate me without a cause wink their eyes.
35:20 For they do not speak of peace, but invent deceitful schemes against those who are quiet in the land.
35:21 They opened their mouths at me and said, 'Aha! we saw it with our own eyes.'
35:22 You saw it, O Lord; do not be silent; O Lord, be not far from me.
35:23 Awake, arise, to my cause! to my defense, my God and my Lord!
35:24 Give me justice, O Lord my God, according to your righteousness; let them not triumph over me.
35:25 Let them not say to themselves, 'Aha! just what we wanted!' nor let them say, 'We have swallowed him up.'
35:26 Let all who rejoice at my ruin be shamed and disgraced; let those who boast against me be clothed with dismay and shame.
35:27 Let those who favor my cause shout for joy and be glad; let them ever say, 'Great is the Lord, who desires the prosperity of his servant.'
35:28 And my tongue shall be talking of your righteousness and of your praise all the day long.

text.KJV:
35:1 Plead my cause, O LORD, with them that strive with me: fight against them that fight against me.
35:2 Take hold of shield and buckler, and stand up for mine help.
35:3 Draw out also the spear, and stop the way against them that persecute me: say unto my soul, I am thy salvation.
35:4 Let them be confounded and put to shame that seek after my soul: let them be turned back and brought to confusion that devise my hurt.
35:5 Let them be as chaff before the wind: and let the angel of the LORD chase them.
35:6 Let their way be dark and slippery: and let the angel of the LORD persecute them.
35:7 For without cause have they hid for me their net in a pit, which without cause they have digged for my soul.
35:8 Let destruction come upon him at unawares; and let his net that he hath hid catch himself: into that very destruction let him fall.
35:9 And my soul shall be joyful in the LORD: it shall rejoice in his salvation.
35:10 All my bones shall say, LORD, who is like unto thee, which deliverest the poor from him that is too strong for him, yea, the poor and the needy from him that spoileth him?
35:11 False witnesses did rise up; they laid to my charge things that I knew not.
35:12 They rewarded me evil for good to the spoiling of my soul.
35:13 But as for me, when they were sick, my clothing was sackcloth: I humbled my soul with fasting; and my prayer returned into mine own bosom.
35:14 I behaved myself as though he had been my friend or brother: I bowed down heavily, as one that mourneth for his mother.
35:15 But in mine adversity they rejoiced, and gathered themselves together: yea, the abjects gathered themselves together against me, and I knew it not; they did tear me, and ceased not:
35:16 With hypocritical mockers in feasts, they gnashed upon me with their teeth.
35:17 Lord, how long wilt thou look on? rescue my soul from their destructions, my darling from the lions.
35:18 I will give thee thanks in the great congregation: I will praise thee among much people.
35:19 Let not them that are mine enemies wrongfully rejoice over me: neither let them wink with the eye that hate me without a cause.
35:20 For they speak not peace: but they devise deceitful matters against them that are quiet in the land.
35:21 Yea, they opened their mouth wide against me, and said, Aha, aha, our eye hath seen it.
35:22 This thou hast seen, O LORD: keep not silence: O Lord, be not far from me.
35:23 Stir up thyself, and awake to my judgment, even unto my cause, my God and my Lord.
35:24 Judge me, O LORD my God, according to thy righteousness; and let them not rejoice over me.
35:25 Let them not say in their hearts, Ah, so would we have it: let them not say, We have swallowed him up.
35:26 Let them be ashamed and brought to confusion together that rejoice at mine hurt: let them be clothed with shame and dishonour that magnify themselves against me.
35:27 Let them shout for joy, and be glad, that favour my righteous cause: yea, let them say continually, Let the LORD be magnified, which hath pleasure in the prosperity of his servant.
35:28 And my tongue shall speak of thy righteousness and of thy praise all the day long.
text.JPS:
35:1 Of David. O LORD, strive with my adversaries, fight those who fight me.
35:2 Take up buckler and shield, and rise to my defense.
35:3 Draw the spear and javelin against my pursuers; say to me, "I am your deliverance."
35:4 Let those who seek my life be frustrated and shamed; let those who plan my ruin back away and be confounded.
35:5 Let them be like chaff before the wind, with the angel of the LORD driving them on.
35:6 Let their path be dark and slippery, with the angel of the LORD pursuing them.
35:7 For without cause they hid a net for me; without cause they dug a pit for my life.
35:8 Let ruin overtake them unawares; let the net they hid catch them, let them fall into ruin.
35:9 Then shall I exult in the LORD, rejoice in His deliverance.
35:10 All my bones shall say, "O LORD, who is like You? You save the lowly from the powerful, the lowly and needy from his despoiler."
35:11 Malicious witnesses come forward; they question me about things I know nothing of.
35:12 They repay me evil for good, bringing bereavement upon me.
35:13 Yet, when they were ill, my dress was sackcloth, I kept a fast... may what I prayed for happen to me!
35:14 I went about as if for my friend or my brother; I cringed in sorrow as one mourning for his mother.
35:15 But when I falter they gather and rejoice; cripples gather against me unawares; they tear at me without ceasing.
35:16 With impious, mocking grimace they gnash their teeth at me.
35:17 O Lord, how long will You look on? Rescue my life from their attacks, my precious life from the lions.
35:18 I will praise You in a great congregation, acclaim You among a mighty throng.
35:19 Let not my treacherous enemies rejoice over me, or those who hate me without cause wink their eyes.
35:20 For they do not speak of amity, but devise deceitful schemes against honest people.
35:21 They open their mouths wide against me; they say, "Aha, aha, our eyes have seen it!"
35:22 You have seen it, O LORD, do not hold Your peace; O Lord, be not far from me.
35:23 Rouse Yourself, wake up for my justice, for my cause, my God and my Lord.
35:24 Vindicate me, O LORD, my God, as befits Your righteousness; let them not rejoice over me.
35:25 Let them not say in their heart, "Aha, just what we wanted!" Let them not say, "We have devoured him!"
35:26 Let those who rejoice at my ruin be frustrated and confounded altogether; let those who vaunt themselves over me be clothed in frustration and shame.
35:27 Let those who desire my vindication sing for joy and rejoice; let them say always, "Extolled be the LORD who desires the well-being of His servant."
35:28 Then my tongue shall utter Your righteousness, Your praise all day long.

text.NRSV:
36:1 There is a voice of rebellion deep in the heart of the wicked; there is no fear of God before his eyes.
36:2 He flatters himself in his own eyes that his abominable sin will not be found out.
36:3 The words of his mouth are wicked and deceitful; he has left off acting wisely and doing good.
36:4 He plots wickedness upon his bed and has set himself in no good way; he does not abhor that which is evil.
36:5 Your love, O Lord, reaches to the heavens, and your faithfulness to the clouds.
36:6 Your righteousness is like the strong mountains, your justice like the great deep; you save both man and beast, O Lord.
36:7 How priceless is your love, O God! your people take refuge under the shadow of your wings.
36:8 They feast upon the abundance of your house; you give them drink from the river of your delights.
36:9 For with you is the well of life, and in your light we see light.
36:10 Continue your loving-kindness to those who know you, and your favor to those who are true of heart.
36:11 Let not the foot of the proud come near me, nor the hand of the wicked push me aside.
36:12 See how they are fallen, those who work wickedness! they are cast down and shall not be able to rise.

text.KJV:
36:1 The transgression of the wicked saith within my heart, that there is no fear of God before his eyes.
36:2 For he flattereth himself in his own eyes, until his iniquity be found to be hateful.
36:3 The words of his mouth are iniquity and deceit: he hath left off to be wise, and to do good.
36:4 He deviseth mischief upon his bed; he setteth himself in a way that is not good; he abhorreth not evil.
36:5 Thy mercy, O LORD, is in the heavens; and thy faithfulness reacheth unto the clouds.
36:6 Thy righteousness is like the great mountains; thy judgments are a great deep: O LORD, thou preservest man and beast.
36:7 How excellent is thy lovingkindness, O God! therefore the children of men put their trust under the shadow of thy wings.
36:8 They shall be abundantly satisfied with the fatness of thy house; and thou shalt make them drink of the river of thy pleasures.
36:9 For with thee is the fountain of life: in thy light shall we see light.
36:10 O continue thy lovingkindness unto them that know thee; and thy righteousness to the upright in heart.
36:11 Let not the foot of pride come against me, and let not the hand of the wicked remove me.
36:12 There are the workers of iniquity fallen: they are cast down, and shall not be able to rise.
text.JPS:
36:1 For the leader. Of David, the servant of the LORD.
36:2 I know what transgression says to the wicked; he has no dread of God.
36:3 Its talk is smooth in his eyes, till his iniquity be found out and detested.
36:4 The words of his mouth are mischief and deceit; he has ceased to be wise, to do good.
36:5 He devises mischief upon his bed; he takes his stand on a path that is not good; he does not reject evil.
36:6 O LORD, Your steadfast love is in heaven; Your faithfulness reaches the skies.
36:7 Your righteousness is like the mighty mountains; Your judgments are like the great deep; man and beast You deliver, O LORD.
36:8 How precious is Your steadfast love, O God! Divine and mortal find refuge in the shadow of Your wings.
36:9 They feast on the abundance of Your house; You give them drink from Your delightful stream.
36:10 With You is the fountain of life; by Your light do we see light.
36:11 Maintain Your steadfast love for those who know You, and Your beneficence for the upright in heart.
36:12 Let not the foot of the arrogant tread on me, or the hand of the wicked drive me away.
36:13 There the evildoers lie fallen; they are thrust down, they cannot rise.

text.NRSV:
37:1 Do not fret yourself because of evildoers; do not be jealous of those who do wrong.
37:2 For they shall soon wither like the grass, and like the green grass fade away.
37:3 Put your trust in the Lord and do good; dwell in the land and feed on its riches.
37:4 Take delight in the Lord, and he shall give you your heart's desire.
37:5 Commit your way to the Lord and put your trust in him, and he will bring it to pass.
37:6 He will make your righteousness as clear as the light and your just dealing as the noonday.
37:7 Be still before the Lord and wait patiently for him.
37:8 Do not fret yourself over the one who prospers, the one who succeeds in evil schemes.
37:9 Refrain from anger, leave rage alone; do not fret yourself; it leads only to evil.
37:10 For evildoers shall be cut off, but those who wait upon the Lord shall possess the land.
37:11 In a little while the wicked shall be no more; you shall search out their place, but they will not be there.
37:12 But the lowly shall possess the land; they will delight in abundance of peace.
37:13 The wicked plot against the righteousness and gnash at them with their teeth.
37:14 The Lord laughs at the wicked, because he sees that their day will come.
37:15 The wicked draw their sword and bend their bow to strike down the poor and needy, to slaughter those who are upright in their ways.
37:16 Their sword shall go through their own heart, and their bow shall be broken.
37:17 Better is the little that the righteous has than the abundance of many wicked.
37:18 For the power of the wicked will be broken, but the Lord upholds the righteous.
37:19 The Lord cares for the lives of the godly, and their inheritance shall last for ever.
37:20 They shall not be ashamed in bad times, and in days of famine they shall have enough.
37:21 As for the wicked, they shall perish, and the enemies of the Lord, like the glory of the meadows, shall vanish; they shall vanish like smoke.
37:22 The wicked borrow and do not repay, but the righteous are generous in giving.
37:23 Those who are blessed by God shall possess the land, but those who are cursed by him shall be destroyed.
37:24 Our steps are directed by the Lord; he strengthens those in whose way he delights.
37:25 If they stumble, they shall not fall headlong, for the Lord holds them by the hand.
37:26 I have been young and now I am old, but never have I seen the righteous forsaken or their children begging bread.
37:27 The righteous are always generous in their lending, and their children shall be a blessing.
37:28 Turn from evil and do good, and dwell in the land for ever.
37:29 For the Lord loves justice; he does not forsake his faithful ones.
37:30 They shall be kept safe for ever, but the offspring of the wicked shall be destroyed.
37:31 The righteous shall possess the land and dwell in it for ever.
37:32 The mouth of the righteous utters wisdom, and their tongue speaks what is right.
37:33 The law of their God is in their heart, and their footsteps shall not falter.
37:34 The wicked spy on the righteous and seek occasion to kill them.
37:35 The Lord will not abandon them to their hand, nor let them be found guilty at their trial.
37:36 Wait upon the Lord and keep his way; he will raise you up to possess the land, and when the wicked are cut off, you will see it.
37:37 I have seen the wicked in their arrogance, flourishing like a tree in full leaf.
37:38 I have passed by, and behold, they were not there; I searched for them, but they could not be found.
37:39 Mark those who are honest; observe the upright; for there is a future for the peaceable.
37:40 Transgressors shall be destroyed, one and all; the future of the wicked is cut off.
37:41 But the deliverance of the righteous comes from the Lord; he is their stronghold in time of trouble.
37:42 The Lord will help them and rescue them; he will rescue them from the wicked and deliver them, because they seek refuge in him.

text.KJV:
37:1 Fret not thyself because of evildoers, neither be thou envious against the workers of iniquity.
37:2 For they shall soon be cut down like the grass, and wither as the green herb.
37:3 Trust in the LORD, and do good; so shalt thou dwell in the land, and verily thou shalt be fed.
37:4 Delight thyself also in the LORD; and he shall give thee the desires of thine heart.
37:5 Commit thy way unto the LORD; trust also in him; and he shall bring it to pass.
37:6 And he shall bring forth thy righteousness as the light, and thy judgment as the noonday.
37:7 Rest in the LORD, and wait patiently for him: fret not thyself because of him who prospereth in his way, because of the man who bringeth wicked devices to pass.
37:8 Cease from anger, and forsake wrath: fret not thyself in any wise to do evil.
37:9 For evildoers shall be cut off: but those that wait upon the LORD, they shall inherit the earth.
37:10 For yet a little while, and the wicked shall not be: yea, thou shalt diligently consider his place, and it shall not be.
37:11 But the meek shall inherit the earth; and shall delight themselves in the abundance of peace.
37:12 The wicked plotteth against the just, and gnasheth upon him with his teeth.
37:13 The Lord shall laugh at him: for he seeth that his day is coming.
37:14 The wicked have drawn out the sword, and have bent their bow, to cast down the poor and needy, and to slay such as be of upright conversation.
37:15 Their sword shall enter into their own heart, and their bows shall be broken.
37:16 A little that a righteous man hath is better than the riches of many wicked.
37:17 For the arms of the wicked shall be broken: but the LORD upholdeth the righteous.
37:18 The LORD knoweth the days of the upright: and their inheritance shall be for ever.
37:19 They shall not be ashamed in the evil time: and in the days of famine they shall be satisfied.
37:20 But the wicked shall perish, and the enemies of the LORD shall be as the fat of lambs: they shall consume; into smoke shall they consume away.
37:21 The wicked borroweth, and payeth not again: but the righteous sheweth mercy, and giveth.
37:22 For such as be blessed of him shall inherit the earth; and they that be cursed of him shall be cut off.
37:23 The steps of a good man are ordered by the LORD: and he delighteth in his way.
37:24 Though he fall, he shall not be utterly cast down: for the LORD upholdeth him with his hand.
37:25 I have been young, and now am old; yet have I not seen the righteous forsaken, nor his seed begging bread.
37:26 He is ever merciful, and lendeth; and his seed is blessed.
37:27 Depart from evil, and do good; and dwell for evermore.
37:28 For the LORD loveth judgment, and forsaketh not his saints; they are preserved for ever: but the seed of the wicked shall be cut off.
37:29 The righteous shall inherit the land, and dwell therein for ever.
37:30 The mouth of the righteous speaketh wisdom, and his tongue talketh of judgment.
37:31 The law of his God is in his heart; none of his steps shall slide.
37:32 The wicked watcheth the righteous, and seeketh to slay him.
37:33 The LORD will not leave him in his hand, nor condemn him when he is judged.
37:34 Wait on the LORD, and keep his way, and he shall exalt thee to inherit the land: when the wicked are cut off, thou shalt see it.
37:35 I have seen the wicked in great power, and spreading himself like a green bay tree.
37:36 Yet he passed away, and, lo, he was not: yea, I sought him, but he could not be found.
37:37 Mark the perfect man, and behold the upright: for the end of that man is peace.
37:38 But the transgressors shall be destroyed together: the end of the wicked shall be cut off.
37:39 But the salvation of the righteous is of the LORD: he is their strength in the time of trouble.
37:40 And the LORD shall help them, and deliver them: he shall deliver them from the wicked, and save them, because they trust in him.
text.JPS:
37:1 Of David. Do not be vexed by evildoers; do not be jealous of wrongdoers;
37:2 for they wither quickly like grass, and fade like the green herb.
37:3 Trust in the LORD and do good; abide in the land and remain loyal.
37:4 Seek your happiness in the LORD, and He will grant you the desires of your heart.
37:5 Commit your way to the LORD; trust in Him and He will act;
37:6 He will cause your vindication to shine forth like the light, and your justice like the noonday sun.
37:7 Be patient for the LORD and wait for Him; do not be vexed by one who prospers in his way, by the man who brings off malicious schemes.
37:8 Desist from anger and forsake fury; do not be vexed, it can only do harm.
37:9 For evildoers shall be cut off, but those who look to the LORD—they shall inherit the land.
37:10 A little longer and the wicked will be no more; look at his place—he will not be there.
37:11 But the lowly shall inherit the land, and delight in abundant well-being.
37:12 The wicked plots against the righteous, and gnashes his teeth at him.
37:13 The Lord laughs at him, for He knows his day is coming.
37:14 The wicked draw the sword and bend their bow, to bring down the lowly and needy, to slaughter those whose way is upright.
37:15 Their sword shall pierce their own heart, and their bows shall be broken.
37:16 Better is the little that the righteous man has than the abundance of many wicked;
37:17 for the arms of the wicked shall be broken, but the LORD supports the righteous.
37:18 The LORD knows the days of the blameless; their inheritance endures forever;
37:19 they will not be shamed in an evil time; in days of famine they will be sated.
37:20 For the wicked shall perish, and the enemies of the LORD shall be like the beauty of the meadows—they vanish, like smoke they vanish away.
37:21 The wicked borrows and does not repay, but the righteous is generous and gives.
37:22 For those blessed by Him shall inherit the land, but those cursed by Him shall be cut off.
37:23 The steps of a man are made firm by the LORD, when He delights in his way;
37:24 though he falter, he shall not be cast down, for the LORD holds his hand.
37:25 I have been young and am now old, but I have never seen a righteous man abandoned, or his children seeking bread.
37:26 He is always generous and lends, and his offspring are a blessing.
37:27 Shun evil and do good, and abide forever.
37:28 For the LORD loves justice and does not abandon His faithful ones. They are preserved forever, but the offspring of the wicked shall be cut off.
37:29 The righteous shall inherit the land, and abide forever in it.
37:30 The mouth of the righteous utters wisdom, and his tongue speaks justice.
37:31 The teaching of his God is in his heart; his feet do not slip.
37:32 The wicked watches for the righteous, seeking to kill him;
37:33 the LORD will not abandon him into his hand, or let him be condemned when he is judged.
37:34 Look to the LORD and keep His way, and He will exalt you to inherit the land; you will look on when the wicked are cut off.
37:35 I have seen a wicked, ruthless man, well-rooted like a robust native tree;
37:36 but he passed away and, lo, he was no more; I sought him and he was not to be found.
37:37 Mark the blameless and watch the upright, for there is a future for the man of amity.
37:38 But transgressors shall be utterly destroyed; the future of the wicked shall be cut off.
37:39 The deliverance of the righteous is from the LORD, their stronghold in time of distress.
37:40 The LORD helps them and rescues them; He rescues them from the wicked and delivers them, for they seek refuge in Him.

text.NRSV:
38:1 O Lord, do not rebuke me in your anger; do not punish me in your wrath.
38:2 For your arrows have already pierced me, and your hand presses hard upon me.
38:3 There is no health in my flesh, because of your indignation; there is no soundness in my body, because of my sin.
38:4 For my iniquities have gone over my head; they are a burden too heavy for me to bear.
38:5 My wounds are foul and festering, because of my foolishness.
38:6 I am bowed down and brought very low; I go mourning all the day long.
38:7 For my loins are filled with burning, and there is no whole part in my body.
38:8 I am feeble and crushed; I roar because of the tumult of my heart.
38:9 Lord, you know all my desires, and my sighing is not hidden from you.
38:10 My heart is pounding, my strength has failed me, and the brightness of my eyes is gone from me.
38:11 My friends and companions stand aloof from my affliction; my neighbors stand far off.
38:12 Those who seek my life lay snares for me; those who strive to hurt me speak of my ruin and plot treachery all the day long.
38:13 But I am like the deaf who do not hear, like those who are mute and do not open their mouth.
38:14 I have become like one who does not hear and from whose mouth comes no defense.
38:15 For in you, O Lord, have I fixed my hope; you will answer me, O Lord my God.
38:16 For I said, 'Let them not rejoice at my expense, those who exult over me when my foot slips.'
38:17 Truly, I am on the verge of falling, and my pain is always with me.
38:18 I will confess my iniquity and be sorry for my sin.
38:19 Those who are my enemies without cause are mighty, and many in number are those who hate me wrongfully.
38:20 Those who repay evil for good slander me, because I follow the course that is right.
38:21 O Lord, do not forsake me; be not far from me, O my God.
38:22 Make haste to help me, O Lord of my salvation.

38:19 Those who are my enemies without cause are mighty, *
      and many in number are those who wrongfully hate me.
38:20 Those who repay evil for good slander me, *
      because I follow the course that is right.
38:21 Forsake me not, O LORD; *
      be not far from me, O my God.
38:22 Make haste to help me, *
      O Lord of my salvation.
text.KJV:
38:1 O LORD, rebuke me not in thy wrath: neither chasten me in thy hot displeasure.
38:2 For thine arrows stick fast in me, and thy hand presseth me sore.
38:3 There is no soundness in my flesh because of thine anger; neither is there any rest in my bones because of my sin.
38:4 For mine iniquities are gone over mine head: as an heavy burden they are too heavy for me.
38:5 My wounds stink and are corrupt because of my foolishness.
38:6 I am troubled; I am bowed down greatly; I go mourning all the day long.
38:7 For my loins are filled with a loathsome disease: and there is no soundness in my flesh.
38:8 I am feeble and sore broken: I have roared by reason of the disquietness of my heart.
38:9 Lord, all my desire is before thee; and my groaning is not hid from thee.
38:10 My heart panteth, my strength faileth me: as for the light of mine eyes, it also is gone from me.
38:11 My lovers and my friends stand aloof from my sore; and my kinsmen stand afar off.
38:12 They also that seek after my life lay snares for me: and they that seek my hurt speak mischievous things, and imagine deceits all the day long.
38:13 But I, as a deaf man, heard not; and I was as a dumb man that openeth not his mouth.
38:14 Thus I was as a man that heareth not, and in whose mouth are no reproofs.
38:15 For in thee, O LORD, do I hope: thou wilt hear, O Lord my God.
38:16 For I said, Hear me, lest otherwise they should rejoice over me: when my foot slippeth, they magnify themselves against me.
38:17 For I am ready to halt, and my sorrow is continually before me.
38:18 For I will declare mine iniquity; I will be sorry for my sin.
38:19 But mine enemies are lively, and they are strong: and they that hate me wrongfully are multiplied.
38:20 They also that render evil for good are mine adversaries; because I follow the thing that good is.
38:21 Forsake me not, O LORD: O my God, be not far from me.
38:22 Make haste to help me, O Lord my salvation.
text.JPS:
38:1 A psalm of David. For the memorial offering.
38:2 O LORD, do not punish me in Your anger, do not chastise me in Your fury.
38:3 For Your arrows have struck me, Your hand has come down upon me.
38:4 There is no soundness in my flesh because of Your rage, no wholeness in my bones because of my sin.
38:5 For my iniquities have overwhelmed me; they are like a heavy burden, more than I can bear.
38:6 My wounds are foul and festering because of my folly.
38:7 I am bent and completely bowed down; all day long I go about in sorrow.
38:8 For my sinews are full of burning; there is no soundness in my flesh.
38:9 I am numb and utterly crushed; I roar because of the turmoil of my heart.
38:10 O Lord, all my desire is before You; my groaning is not hidden from You.
38:11 My heart flutters, my strength fails me; the light of my eyes also is gone from me.
38:12 My friends and companions stand aloof from my plague, and my kinsmen stand far off.
38:13 Those who seek my life lay snares, and those who desire my ruin speak of malice, and utter deceit all day long.
38:14 But I am like a deaf man, I do not hear; I am like a dumb man who does not open his mouth.
38:15 I am like a man who does not hear, and has no rebukes in his mouth.
38:16 For in You, O LORD, I look; You will answer, O Lord my God.
38:17 For I said, "Lest they rejoice over me, vaunting themselves over me when my foot slips."
38:18 For I am ready to limp, and my sorrow is ever before me.
38:19 For I acknowledge my iniquity; I am fearful because of my sin.
38:20 But my mortal enemies are powerful, and those who hate me without cause are numerous.
38:21 Those who repay evil for good harass me because I pursue good.
38:22 Do not abandon me, O LORD; my God, be not far from me.
38:23 Hasten to my aid, O Lord, my deliverance.

text.NRSV:
39:1 I said, 'I will keep watch upon my ways, so that I do not offend with my tongue.'
39:2 I will put a muzzle on my mouth while the wicked are in my presence.
39:3 So I held my tongue and said nothing; I kept silent but to no avail, and my distress was increased.
39:4 My heart grew hot within me; while I pondered, the fire burst into flame; I spoke out with my tongue:
39:5 'Lord, let me know my end and the number of my days, so that I may know how short my life is.
39:6 You have given me a mere handful of days, and my lifetime is as nothing in your sight; truly, even those who stand erect are but a puff of wind.
39:7 We walk about like a shadow, and in vain we are in turmoil; we heap up riches and cannot tell who will gather them.
39:8 And now, Lord, what is my hope? my hope is in you.
39:9 Deliver me from all my transgressions and do not make me the taunt of the fool.
39:10 I fell silent and did not open my mouth, for surely it was you who did it.
39:11 Take your affliction from me; I am consumed by the blows of your hand.
39:12 With rebukes for sin you punish us; like a moth you eat away all that is dear to us; truly, everyone is but a puff of wind.
39:13 Hear my prayer, O Lord, and give ear to my cry; be not silent at my tears.
39:14 For I am a stranger with you, a sojourner, as all my forefathers were.
39:15 Turn your gaze from me, that I may be glad again, before I go my way and am no more.

      so that I do not offend with my tongue.
39:2 I will put a muzzle on my mouth *
      while the wicked are in my presence."
39:3 So I held my tongue and said nothing; *
      I refrained from rash words;
      but my pain became unbearable.
39:4 My heart was hot within me;
      while I pondered, the fire burst into flame; *
      I spoke out with my tongue:
39:5 LORD, let me know my end
      and the number of my days, *
      so that I may know how short my life is.
39:6 You have given me a mere handful of days,
      and my lifetime is as nothing in your sight; *
      truly, even those who stand erect are but a puff of wind.
39:7 We walk about like a shadow,
      and in vain we are in turmoil; *
      we heap up riches and cannot tell who will gather them.
39:8 And now, what is my hope? *
      O Lord, my hope is in you.
39:9 Deliver me from all my transgressions *
      and do not make me the taunt of the fool.
39:10 I fell silent and did not open my mouth, *
      for surely it was you that did it.
39:11 Take your affliction from me; *
      I am worn down by the blows of your hand.
39:12 With rebukes for sin you punish us;
      like a moth you eat away all that is dear to us; *
      truly, everyone is but a puff of wind.
39:13 Hear my prayer, O LORD,
      and give ear to my cry; *
      hold not your peace at my tears.
39:14 For I am but a sojourner with you, *
      a wayfarer, as all my forebears were.
39:15 Turn your gaze from me, that I may be glad again, *
      before I go my way and am no more.
text.KJV:
39:1 I said, I will take heed to my ways, that I sin not with my tongue: I will keep my mouth with a bridle, while the wicked is before me.
39:2 I was dumb with silence, I held my peace, even from good; and my sorrow was stirred.
39:3 My heart was hot within me, while I was musing the fire burned: then spake I with my tongue,
39:4 LORD, make me to know mine end, and the measure of my days, what it is; that I may know how frail I am.
39:5 Behold, thou hast made my days as an handbreadth; and mine age is as nothing before thee: verily every man at his best state is altogether vanity. Selah.
39:6 Surely every man walketh in a vain shew: surely they are disquieted in vain: he heapeth up riches, and knoweth not who shall gather them.
39:7 And now, Lord, what wait I for? my hope is in thee.
39:8 Deliver me from all my transgressions: make me not the reproach of the foolish.
39:9 I was dumb, I opened not my mouth; because thou didst it.
39:10 Remove thy stroke away from me: I am consumed by the blow of thine hand.
39:11 When thou with rebukes dost correct man for iniquity, thou makest his beauty to consume away like a moth: surely every man is vanity. Selah.
39:12 Hear my prayer, O LORD, and give ear unto my cry; hold not thy peace at my tears: for I am a stranger with thee, and a sojourner, as all my fathers were.
39:13 O spare me, that I may recover strength, before I go hence, and be no more.
text.JPS:
39:1 For the leader; for Jeduthun. A psalm of David.
39:2 I said, "I will guard my ways, that I may not sin with my tongue; I will guard my mouth with a muzzle, as long as the wicked is in my presence."
39:3 I was dumb, silent; I was still, away from good; and my sorrow was stirred.
39:4 My heart grew hot within me; as I pondered, the fire blazed; then I spoke with my tongue:
39:5 "O LORD, let me know my end, and what is the measure of my days; let me know how fleeting my life is.
39:6 You have made my days a few handbreadths, and my lifetime is as nothing before You; surely every man stands as a mere breath. Selah.
39:7 Surely man goes about as a shadow; surely they are in turmoil for nothing; he heaps up wealth, and does not know who will gather it.
39:8 And now, O Lord, what do I look for? My hope is in You.
39:9 Deliver me from all my transgressions; do not make me the scorn of the fool.
39:10 I am dumb, I do not open my mouth, for it is You who have acted.
39:11 Remove Your plague from me; I am consumed by the blow of Your hand.
39:12 You chastise man with rebukes for sin, and consume like a moth what is dear to him; surely every man is a mere breath. Selah.
39:13 Hear my prayer, O LORD, and give ear to my cry; do not hold Your peace at my tears; for I am a stranger with You, a sojourner, like all my fathers.
39:14 Look away from me, that I may smile again, before I depart and am no more."

text.NRSV:
40:1 I waited patiently upon the Lord; he stooped to me and heard my cry.
40:2 He lifted me out of the desolate pit, out of the mire and clay; he set my feet upon a high cliff and made my footing sure.
40:3 He put a new song in my mouth, a song of praise to our God; many shall see, and stand in awe, and put their trust in the Lord.
40:4 Happy are they who trust in the Lord! they do not resort to evil spirits or turn to false gods.
40:5 Great things are they that you have done, O Lord my God! how great your wonders and your plans for us! there is none who can be compared with you.
40:6 Oh, that I could make them known and tell them! but they are more than I can count.
40:7 In sacrifice and offering you take no pleasure (you have given me ears to hear you);
40:8 Burnt-offering and sin-offering you have not required, and so I said, 'Behold, I come.
40:9 In the roll of the book it is written concerning me: 'I love to do your will, O my God; your law is deep in my heart.'
40:10 I proclaimed righteousness in the great assembly; behold, I did not restrain my lips; and that, O Lord, you know.
40:11 Your righteousness have I not hidden in my heart; I have spoken of your faithfulness and your deliverance; I have not concealed your love and faithfulness from the great assembly.
40:12 You are the Lord; do not withhold your compassion from me; let your love and your faithfulness keep me safe for ever,
40:13 For innumerable troubles have crowded upon me; my sins have overtaken me, and I cannot see; they are more in number than the hairs of my head, and my heart fails me.
40:14 Be pleased, O Lord, to deliver me; O Lord, make haste to help me.
40:15 Let those who seek my life to destroy it be humbled and confounded altogether; let those who take pleasure in my misfortune draw back and be disgraced.
40:16 Let those who say to me 'Aha!' and gloat over me be desolate because of their shame.
40:17 Let all who seek you rejoice in you and be glad; let those who love your salvation say for ever, 'Great is the Lord!'
40:18 Though I am poor and afflicted, the Lord will have care for me.
40:19 You are my helper and my deliverer; do not tarry, O my God.

text.KJV:
40:1 I waited patiently for the LORD; and he inclined unto me, and heard my cry.
40:2 He brought me up also out of an horrible pit, out of the miry clay, and set my feet upon a rock, and established my goings.
40:3 And he hath put a new song in my mouth, even praise unto our God: many shall see it, and fear, and shall trust in the LORD.
40:4 Blessed is that man that maketh the LORD his trust, and respecteth not the proud, nor such as turn aside to lies.
40:5 Many, O LORD my God, are thy wonderful works which thou hast done, and thy thoughts which are to us-ward: they cannot be reckoned up in order unto thee: if I would declare and speak of them, they are more than can be numbered.
40:6 Sacrifice and offering thou didst not desire; mine ears hast thou opened: burnt offering and sin offering hast thou not required.
40:7 Then said I, Lo, I come: in the volume of the book it is written of me,
40:8 I delight to do thy will, O my God: yea, thy law is within my heart.
40:9 I have preached righteousness in the great congregation: lo, I have not refrained my lips, O LORD, thou knowest.
40:10 I have not hid thy righteousness within my heart; I have declared thy faithfulness and thy salvation: I have not concealed thy lovingkindness and thy truth from the great congregation.
40:11 Withhold not thou thy tender mercies from me, O LORD: let thy lovingkindness and thy truth continually preserve me.
40:12 For innumerable evils have compassed me about: mine iniquities have taken hold upon me, so that I am not able to look up; they are more than the hairs of mine head: therefore my heart faileth me.
40:13 Be pleased, O LORD, to deliver me: O LORD, make haste to help me.
40:14 Let them be ashamed and confounded together that seek after my soul to destroy it; let them be driven backward and put to shame that wish me evil.
40:15 Let them be desolate for a reward of their shame that say unto me, Aha, aha.
40:16 Let all those that seek thee rejoice and be glad in thee: let such as love thy salvation say continually, The LORD be magnified.
40:17 But I am poor and needy; yet the Lord thinketh upon me: thou art my help and my deliverer; make no tarrying, O my God.
text.JPS:
40:1 For the leader. Of David. A psalm.
40:2 I looked intently to the LORD; He turned to me and heard my cry.
40:3 He lifted me up from the tumultuous pit, from the miry clay, and set my feet upon a rock, making my steps firm.
40:4 He put a new song in my mouth, a hymn to our God; many shall see it and fear, and trust in the LORD.
40:5 Happy is the man who makes the LORD his trust, and does not turn to the arrogant or to those who fall into falsehood.
40:6 You have done many things, O LORD my God, Your wonders and Your thoughts toward us; none can compare to You; were I to report and speak of them, they are more than can be told.
40:7 Sacrifice and meal-offering You did not desire; You have opened ears for me; burnt-offering and sin-offering You did not ask.
40:8 Then I said, "See, I come; in the scroll of the book it is prescribed for me.
40:9 To do Your will, my God, is my desire; Your teaching is in my inmost parts."
40:10 I heralded righteousness in a great congregation; see, I did not curb my lips, O LORD, You know it.
40:11 I did not hide Your righteousness within my heart; I spoke of Your faithfulness and Your deliverance; I did not conceal Your steadfast love and Your truth from the great congregation.
40:12 You, O LORD, will not curb Your compassion from me; Your steadfast love and Your truth will always guard me.
40:13 For evils beyond number have encompassed me; my iniquities have overtaken me, and I cannot see; they are more than the hairs of my head, and my heart fails me.
40:14 Be pleased, O LORD, to rescue me; O LORD, hasten to my aid.
40:15 Let those who seek to sweep away my life be frustrated and confounded together; let those who desire my ruin back away and be shamed.
40:16 Let those who say to me, "Aha, aha!" be desolate because of their shame.
40:17 Let all who seek You rejoice and be glad in You; let those who love Your deliverance say always, "Extolled be the LORD."
40:18 But I am lowly and needy; may the Lord design for me. You are my help and my deliverer; my God, do not tarry.

text.NRSV:
41:1 Happy are they who consider the poor and needy! the Lord will deliver them in the time of trouble.
41:2 The Lord preserves them and keeps them alive, so that they may be happy in the land; he does not hand them over to the will of their enemies.
41:3 The Lord sustains them on their sickbed and ministers to them in their illness.
41:4 I said, 'Lord, be merciful to me; heal me, for I have sinned against you.'
41:5 My enemies are saying wicked things about me: 'When will he die, and his name perish?'
41:6 Even if they come to see me, they speak empty words; their heart collects false rumors; they go outside and spread them.
41:7 All my enemies whisper together about me and devise evil against me.
41:8 'A deadly thing,' they say, 'has fastened on him; he has taken to his bed and will never get up again.'
41:9 Even my best friend, whom I trusted, who broke bread with me, has lifted up his heel and turned against me.
41:10 But you, O Lord, be merciful to me and raise me up, and I shall repay them.
41:11 By this I know you are pleased with me, that my enemy does not triumph over me.
41:12 In my integrity you hold me fast, and shall set me before your face for ever.
41:13 Blessed be the Lord, the God of Israel, from age to age. Amen. Amen.

text.KJV:
41:1 Blessed is he that considereth the poor: the LORD will deliver him in time of trouble.
41:2 The LORD will preserve him, and keep him alive; and he shall be blessed upon the earth: and thou wilt not deliver him unto the will of his enemies.
41:3 The LORD will strengthen him upon the bed of languishing: thou wilt make all his bed in his sickness.
41:4 I said, LORD, be merciful unto me: heal my soul; for I have sinned against thee.
41:5 Mine enemies speak evil of me, When shall he die, and his name perish?
41:6 And if he come to see me, he speaketh vanity: his heart gathereth iniquity to itself; when he goeth abroad, he telleth it.
41:7 All that hate me whisper together against me: against me do they devise my hurt.
41:8 An evil disease, say they, cleaveth fast unto him: and now that he lieth he shall rise up no more.
41:9 Yea, mine own familiar friend, in whom I trusted, which did eat of my bread, hath lifted up his heel against me.
41:10 But thou, O LORD, be merciful unto me, and raise me up, that I may requite them.
41:11 By this I know that thou favourest me, because mine enemy doth not triumph over me.
41:12 And as for me, thou upholdest me in mine integrity, and settest me before thy face for ever.
41:13 Blessed be the LORD God of Israel from everlasting, and to everlasting. Amen, and Amen.
text.JPS:
41:1 For the leader. A psalm of David.
41:2 Happy is he who considers the poor; the LORD will deliver him on an evil day.
41:3 The LORD will guard him and keep him alive; he shall be happy in the land; You will not give him up to the will of his enemies.
41:4 The LORD will sustain him upon his bed of illness; You turn his bed completely when he is sick.
41:5 As for me, I said, "O LORD, have mercy on me; heal my soul, for I have sinned against You."
41:6 My enemies speak evil of me: "When will he die, and his name perish?"
41:7 And if one comes to see me, he speaks falsehood; his heart gathers malice to itself; when he goes outside, he talks.
41:8 All who hate me whisper together against me; they devise mischief against me:
41:9 "A vile thing is poured out upon him; now that he lies down, he will rise up no more."
41:10 Even my close friend in whom I trusted, who ate my bread, has lifted his heel against me.
41:11 But You, O LORD, have mercy on me and raise me up, that I may requite them.
41:12 By this I know that You delight in me, that my enemy does not vaunt himself over me.
41:13 As for me, because of my integrity You support me, and set me in Your presence forever.
41:14 Blessed is the LORD, the God of Israel, from everlasting to everlasting. Amen and Amen.

text.NRSV:
42:1 As the deer longs for the water-brooks, so longs my soul for you, O God.
42:2 My soul is athirst for God, athirst for the living God; when shall I come to appear before the presence of God?
42:3 My tears have been my food day and night, while all day long they say to me, 'Where now is your God?'
42:4 I pour out my soul when I think on these things: how I went with the multitude and led them into the house of God,
42:5 With the voice of praise and thanksgiving, among those who keep holy-day.
42:6 Why are you so full of heaviness, O my soul? and why are you so disquieted within me?
42:7 Put your trust in God; for I will yet give thanks to him, who is the help of my countenance, and my God.
42:8 My soul is heavy within me; therefore I will remember you from the land of Jordan, and from the peak of Mizar among the heights of Hermon.
42:9 One deep calls to another in the noise of your cataracts; all your rapids and floods have gone over me.
42:10 The Lord grants his loving-kindness in the daytime; in the night season his song is with me, a prayer to the God of my life.
42:11 I will say to the God of my strength, 'Why have you forgotten me? and why do I go so heavily while the enemy oppresses me?'
42:12 While my bones are being broken, my enemies mock me to my face;
42:13 All day long they mock me and say to me, 'Where now is your God?'
42:14 Why are you so full of heaviness, O my soul? and why are you so disquieted within me?
42:15 Put your trust in God; for I will yet give thanks to him, who is the help of my countenance, and my God.

text.KJV:
42:1 As the hart panteth after the water brooks, so panteth my soul after thee, O God.
42:2 My soul thirsteth for God, for the living God: when shall I come and appear before God?
42:3 My tears have been my meat day and night, while they continually say unto me, Where is thy God?
42:4 When I remember these things, I pour out my soul in me: for I had gone with the multitude, I went with them to the house of God, with the voice of joy and praise, with a multitude that kept holyday.
42:5 Why art thou cast down, O my soul? and why art thou disquieted in me? hope thou in God: for I shall yet praise him for the help of his countenance.
42:6 O my God, my soul is cast down within me: therefore will I remember thee from the land of Jordan, and of the Hermonites, from the hill Mizar.
42:7 Deep calleth unto deep at the noise of thy waterspouts: all thy waves and thy billows are gone over me.
42:8 Yet the LORD will command his lovingkindness in the daytime, and in the night his song shall be with me, and my prayer unto the God of my life.
42:9 I will say unto God my rock, Why hast thou forgotten me? why go I mourning because of the oppression of the enemy?
42:10 As with a sword in my bones, mine enemies reproach me; while they say daily unto me, Where is thy God?
42:11 Why art thou cast down, O my soul? and why art thou disquieted within me? hope thou in God: for I shall yet praise him, who is the health of my countenance, and my God.
text.JPS:
42:1 For the leader. A maskil of the sons of Korah.
42:2 As a hind pants for flowing streams, so my soul pants for You, O God.
42:3 My soul thirsteth for God, for the living God; when shall I come and appear before God?
42:4 My tears have been my food day and night, while they say to me all day long, "Where is your God?"
42:5 These things I remember, and pour out my soul within me: how I passed along with the throng, leading them in procession to the house of God, with the voice of joy and praise, a crowd keeping holiday.
42:6 Why are you bowed down, O my soul, and why are you disquieted within me? Look to God; for I shall yet praise Him for the deliverances of His presence.
42:7 O my God, my soul is bowed down within me; therefore I remember You from the land of Jordan, and the Hermons, from Mount Mizar.
42:8 Deep calls to deep at the thunder of Your cataracts; all Your breakers and Your waves have passed over me.
42:9 By day the LORD will command His steadfast love, and at night His song shall be with me, a prayer to the God of my life.
42:10 I say to God, my Rock, "Why have You forgotten me? Why do I go about in sorrow because of the oppression of the enemy?"
42:11 As with a crushing in my bones, my adversaries mock me, while they say to me all day long, "Where is your God?"
42:12 Why are you bowed down, O my soul, and why are you disquieted within me? Look to God; for I shall yet praise Him, the deliverance of my countenance, and my God.

text.NRSV:
43:1 Give judgment for me, O God, and defend my cause against an ungodly people; deliver me from the deceitful and the wicked.
43:2 For you are the God of my strength; why have you put me from you? and why do I go so heavily while the enemy oppresses me?
43:3 Send out your light and your truth, that they may lead me, and bring me to your holy hill and to your dwelling;
43:4 That I may go to the altar of God, to the God of my joy and gladness; and on the harp I will give thanks to you, O God my God.
43:5 Why are you so full of heaviness, O my soul? and why are you so disquieted within me?
43:6 Put your trust in God; for I will yet give thanks to him, who is the help of my countenance, and my God.

text.KJV:

43:2 For thou art the God of my strength: why dost thou cast me off? why go I mourning because of the oppression of the enemy?
43:3 O send out thy light and thy truth: let them lead me; let them bring me unto thy holy hill, and to thy tabernacles.

text.JPS:
43:1 Vindicate me, O God, and strive for my cause against an ungodly nation; rescue me from the deceitful and lawless man.
43:2 For You are the God of my stronghold; why have You cast me off? Why do I go about in sorrow because of the oppression of the enemy?
43:3 Send out Your light and Your truth; let them lead me; let them bring me to Your holy mountain and to Your tabernacles.
43:4 Then I will go to the altar of God, to God the joy of my exultation; and I will praise You with the lyre, O God, my God.
43:5 Why are you bowed down, O my soul, and why are you disquieted within me? Look to God; for I shall yet praise Him, the deliverance of my countenance, and my God.

text.NRSV:
44:1 We have heard with our ears, O God, our forefathers have told us, all the things you did in their days,
44:2 How you drove out the nations with your hand and planted them; how you afflicted the peoples and made them flourish.
44:3 For they did not take the land by their sword, nor did their arm win the victory for them; but your right hand, your arm, and the light of your countenance, because you favored them.
44:4 You are my King and my God; you command victories for Jacob.
44:5 Through you we pushed back our adversaries; through your Name we trampled on those who rose up against us.
44:6 For I do not rely on my bow, and my sword does not give me the victory.
44:7 Surely you gave us victory over our adversaries and put those who hate us to shame.
44:8 Every day we gloried in God, and we will praise your Name for ever.
44:9 Nevertheless, you have rejected and humbled us and do not go forth with our armies.
44:10 You have made us fall back before our adversary, and our enemies have plundered us.
44:11 You have made us like sheep to be eaten and have scattered us among the nations.
44:12 You are selling your people for a trifle and are making no profit on the sale.
44:13 You have made us the scorn of our neighbors, a mockery and derision to those around us.
44:14 You have made us a byword among the nations, a laughingstock among the peoples.
44:15 All day long my disgrace is before me, and shame has covered my face;
44:16 At the sound of the taunter and reviler, and at the sight of the enemy and avenger.
44:17 All this has come upon us; yet we have not forgotten you, nor have we betrayed your covenant.
44:18 Our heart has not turned back, nor have our steps strayed from your path;
44:19 Though you would cast us out among wild beasts and cover us with the shadow of death.
44:20 If we have forgotten the Name of our God, or stretched out our hands to some strange god,
44:21 Will not God find it out? for he knows the secrets of the heart.
44:22 Indeed, for your sake we are killed all the day long; we are accounted as sheep for the slaughter.
44:23 Awake, O Lord! why are you sleeping? Arise and do not reject us for ever.
44:24 Why do you hide your face and forget our affliction and oppression?
44:25 For we sink down into the dust; our body cleaves to the ground.
44:26 Rise up, help us, and save us, for the sake of your steadfast love.

text.KJV:
44:1 We have heard with our ears, O God, our fathers have told us, what work thou didst in their days, in the times of old.
44:2 How thou didst drive out the heathen with thy hand, and plantedst them; how thou didst afflict the people, and cast them out.
44:3 For they got not the land in possession by their own sword, neither did their own arm save them: but thy right hand, and thine arm, and the light of thy countenance, because thou hadst a favour unto them.
44:4 Thou art my King, O God: command deliverances for Jacob.
44:5 Through thee will we push down our enemies: through thy name will we tread them under that rise up against us.
44:6 For I will not trust in my bow, neither shall my sword save me.
44:7 But thou hast saved us from our enemies, and hast put them to shame that hated us.
44:8 In God we boast all the day long, and praise thy name for ever. Selah.
44:9 But thou hast cast off, and put us to shame; and goest not forth with our armies.
44:10 Thou makest us to turn back from the enemy: and they which hate us spoil for themselves.
44:11 Thou hast given us like sheep appointed for meat; and hast scattered us among the heathen.
44:12 Thou sellest thy people for nought, and dost not increase thy wealth by their price.
44:13 Thou makest us a reproach to our neighbours, a scorn and a derision to them that are round about us.
44:14 Thou makest us a byword among the heathen, a shaking of the head among the people.
44:15 My confusion is continually before me, and the shame of my face hath covered me,
44:16 For the voice of him that reproacheth and blasphemeth; by reason of the enemy and avenger.
44:17 All this is come upon us; yet have we not forgotten thee, neither have we dealt falsely in thy covenant.
44:18 Our heart is not turned back, neither have our steps declined from thy way;
44:19 Though thou hast sore broken us in the place of dragons, and covered us with the shadow of death.
44:20 If we have forgotten the name of our God, or stretched out our hands to a strange god;
44:21 Shall not God search this out? for he knoweth the secrets of the heart.
44:22 Yea, for thy sake are we killed all the day long; we are counted as sheep for the slaughter.
44:23 Awake, why sleepest thou, O Lord? arise, cast us not off for ever.
44:24 Wherefore hidest thou thy face, and forgettest our affliction and our oppression?
44:25 For our soul is bowed down to the dust: our belly cleaveth unto the earth.
44:26 Arise for our help, and redeem us for thy mercies' sake.
text.JPS:
44:1 For the leader. Of the sons of Korah. A maskil.
44:2 We have heard with our ears, O God, our fathers have told us, what work You did in their days, in the days of old.
44:3 You with Your hand did drive out nations, and planted them; You did afflict peoples, and set them free.
44:4 For they did not inherit the land by their own sword, nor did their own arm deliver them; but Your right hand, and Your arm, and the light of Your countenance, because You favored them.
44:5 You are my King, O God; command deliverances for Jacob.
44:6 Through You we will push down our adversaries; through Your name we will tread down those who rise up against us.
44:7 For I will not trust in my bow, nor shall my sword deliver me.
44:8 For You have delivered us from our adversaries, and have shamed those who hated us.
44:9 In God we have boasted all day long, and Your name we will praise forever. Selah.
44:10 Yet You have cast us off and shamed us, and do not go forth with our armies.
44:11 You make us turn back from the adversary, and those who hate us plunder for themselves.
44:12 You have given us up like sheep for food, and have scattered us among the nations.
44:13 You sell Your people for nothing, and set no high price on them.
44:14 You make us a reproach to our neighbors, a scorn and a derision to those around us.
44:15 You make us a byword among the nations, a shaking of the head among the peoples.
44:16 All day long my confusion is before me, and the shame of my face covers me,
44:17 because of the voice of him who reproaches and reviles, because of the enemy and the avenger.
44:18 All this is come upon us, yet we have not forgotten You, nor have we dealt falsely in Your covenant.
44:19 Our heart is not turned back, nor have our steps declined from Your path,
44:20 though You have crushed us in the place of jackals, and covered us with the shadow of death.
44:21 If we have forgotten the name of our God, or stretched out our hands to a strange god,
44:22 will not God search this out? For He knows the secrets of the heart.
44:23 Yea, for Your sake we are killed all day long; we are counted as sheep for the slaughter.
44:24 Rouse Yourself! Why do You sleep, O Lord? Wake up! Do not cast us off forever.
44:25 Why do You hide Your face, forgetting our affliction and our oppression?
44:26 For our soul is bowed down to the dust; our belly cleaves to the earth.
44:27 Rise up for our help, and redeem us for Your steadfast love's sake.

text.NRSV:
45:1 My heart is stirring with a noble song; let me recite what I have fashioned for the king; my tongue shall be the pen of a skilled writer.
45:2 You are the fairest of men; grace flows from your lips, because God has blessed you for ever.
45:3 Strap your sword upon your thigh, O mighty warrior, in your pride and in your majesty.
45:4 Ride out and conquer in the cause of truth and for the sake of justice; your right hand shall show you marvelous things.
45:5 Your arrows are very sharp, O mighty warrior; the peoples are falling at your feet, and the king's enemies are losing heart.
45:6 Your throne, O God, endures for ever and ever, a scepter of righteousness is the scepter of your kingdom; you love righteousness and hate iniquity.
45:7 Therefore God, your God, has anointed you with the oil of gladness above your fellows.
45:8 All your garments are fragrant with myrrh, aloes, and cassia, and the music of strings from ivory palaces makes you glad.
45:9 Kings' daughters stand among the ladies of the court; on your right hand is the queen, adorned with the gold of Ophir.
45:10 'Hear, O daughter; consider and listen closely; forget your people and your father's house.
45:11 The king will have pleasure in your beauty; he is your master; therefore do him honor.
45:12 The people of Tyre are here with a gift; the rich among the people seek your favor.'
45:13 All glorious is the princess as she enters; her gown is cloth-of-gold.
45:14 In embroidered apparel she is brought to the king; after her the bridesmaids follow in procession.
45:15 With joy and gladness they are brought, and they enter into the palace of the king.
45:16 'In place of fathers, O king, you shall have sons; you shall make them princes over all the earth.
45:17 I will make your name to be remembered from one generation to another; therefore nations will praise you for ever and ever.'

text.KJV:
45:1 My heart is inditing a good matter: I speak of the things which I have made touching the king: my tongue is the pen of a ready writer.
45:2 Thou art fairer than the children of men: grace is poured into thy lips: therefore God hath blessed thee for ever.
45:3 Gird thy sword upon thy thigh, O most mighty, with thy glory and thy majesty.
45:4 And in thy majesty ride prosperously because of truth and meekness and righteousness; and thy right hand shall teach thee terrible things.
45:5 Thine arrows are sharp in the heart of the king's enemies; whereby the people fall under thee.
45:6 Thy throne, O God, is for ever and ever: the sceptre of thy kingdom is a right sceptre.
45:7 Thou lovest righteousness, and hatest wickedness: therefore God, thy God, hath anointed thee with the oil of gladness above thy fellows.
45:8 All thy garments smell of myrrh, and aloes, and cassia, out of the ivory palaces, whereby they have made thee glad.
45:9 Kings' daughters were among thy honourable women: upon thy right hand did stand the queen in gold of Ophir.
45:10 Hearken, O daughter, and consider, and incline thine ear; forget also thine own people, and thy father's house;
45:11 So shall the king greatly desire thy beauty: for he is thy Lord; and worship thou him.
45:12 And the daughter of Tyre shall be there with a gift; even the rich among the people shall intreat thy favour.
45:13 The king's daughter is all glorious within: her clothing is of wrought gold.
45:14 She shall be brought unto the king in raiment of needlework: the virgins her companions that follow her shall be brought unto thee.
45:15 With gladness and rejoicing shall they be brought: they shall enter into the king's palace.
45:16 Instead of thy fathers shall be thy children, whom thou mayest make princes in all the earth.
45:17 I will make thy name to be remembered in all generations: therefore shall the people praise thee for ever and ever.
text.JPS:
45:1 For the leader; upon Shoshannim. Of the sons of Korah. A maskil. A song of loves.
45:2 My heart overflows with a goodly matter; I speak my verses concerning a king; my tongue is the pen of a ready writer.
45:3 You are fairer than the children of men; grace is poured upon your lips; therefore God has blessed you forever.
45:4 Gird your sword upon your thigh, O mighty one, your glory and your majesty.
45:5 And in your majesty ride on prosperously, because of truth and meekness and righteousness; and let your right hand teach you terrible things.
45:6 Your arrows are sharp; peoples fall under you; they pierce the heart of the king's enemies.
45:7 Your throne, given of God, endures forever and ever; a scepter of equity is the scepter of your kingdom.
45:8 You have loved righteousness and hated wickedness; therefore God, your God, has anointed you with the oil of gladness above your companions.
45:9 All your garments smell of myrrh, aloes, and cassia; out of ivory palaces stringed instruments have made you glad.
45:10 Kings' daughters are among your honorable women; at your right hand stands the queen in gold of Ophir.
45:11 Hearken, O daughter, and consider, and incline your ear; forget also your own people and your father's house;
45:12 so shall the king desire your beauty; for he is your lord, and bow down to him.
45:13 And the daughter of Tyre shall come with a gift; the rich among the people shall entreat your favor.
45:14 All glorious is the king's daughter within the palace; her clothing is interwoven with gold.
45:15 She shall be led to the king in richly woven apparel; the virgins her companions that follow her shall be brought to you.
45:16 With gladness and rejoicing shall they be led; they shall enter into the king's palace.
45:17 Instead of your fathers shall be your children, whom you may make princes in all the earth.
45:18 I will make your name to be remembered in all generations; therefore shall the peoples praise you forever and ever.

text.NRSV:
46:1 God is our refuge and strength, a very present help in trouble.
46:2 Therefore we will not fear, though the earth be moved, and though the mountains be shaken into the depths of the sea;
46:3 Though its waters rage and foam, and though the mountains tremble at its tumult.
46:4 There is a river whose streams make glad the city of God, the holy habitation of the Most High.
46:5 God is in the midst of her; she shall not be overthrown; God shall help her at the break of day.
46:6 The nations make much ado, and the kingdoms are shaken; God has uttered his voice, and the earth shall melt away.
46:7 The Lord of hosts is with us; the God of Jacob is our stronghold.
46:8 Come now and look upon the works of the Lord, what awesome things he has done on earth.
46:9 It is he who makes war to cease in all the world; he breaks the bow, and shatters the spear, and burns the shields with fire.
46:10 'Be still, then, and know that I am God; I will be exalted among the nations; I will be exalted in the earth.'
46:11 The Lord of hosts is with us; the God of Jacob is our stronghold.

text.KJV:
46:1 God is our refuge and strength, a very present help in trouble.
46:2 Therefore will not we fear, though the earth be removed, and though the mountains be carried into the midst of the sea;
46:3 Though the waters thereof roar and be troubled, though the mountains shake with the swelling thereof. Selah.
46:4 There is a river, the streams whereof shall make glad the city of God, the holy place of the tabernacles of the most High.
46:5 God is in the midst of her; she shall not be moved: God shall help her, and that right early.
46:6 The heathen raged, the kingdoms were moved: he uttered his voice, the earth melted.
46:7 The LORD of hosts is with us; the God of Jacob is our refuge. Selah.
46:8 Come, behold the works of the LORD, what desolations he hath made in the earth.
46:9 He maketh wars to cease unto the end of the earth; he breaketh the bow, and cutteth the spear in sunder; he burneth the chariot in the fire.
46:10 Be still, and know that I am God: I will be exalted among the heathen, I will be exalted in the earth.
46:11 The LORD of hosts is with us; the God of Jacob is our refuge. Selah.
text.JPS:
46:1 For the leader. Of the sons of Korah. Upon Alamoth. A song.
46:2 God is our refuge and strength, a very present help in trouble.
46:3 Therefore we will not fear, though the earth do change, and though the mountains slip into the heart of the seas;
46:4 though the waters thereof roar and foam, though the mountains shake with the swelling thereof. Selah.
46:5 There is a river, the streams whereof make glad the city of God, the holy place of the tabernacles of the Most High.
46:6 God is in the midst of her, she shall not be shaken; God shall help her at the dawn of morning.
46:7 Nations raged, kingdoms were shaken; He uttered His voice, the earth melted.
46:8 The LORD of hosts is with us; the God of Jacob is our high tower. Selah.
46:9 Come, behold the works of the LORD, what desolations He has made in the earth.
46:10 He makes wars to cease unto the end of the earth; He breaks the bow, and cuts the spear in sunder; He burns the chariots in the fire.
46:11 Let be, and know that I am God; I will be exalted among the nations, I will be exalted in the earth.
46:12 The LORD of hosts is with us; the God of Jacob is our high tower. Selah.

text.NRSV:
47:1 Clap your hands, all you peoples; shout to God with a cry of joy.
47:2 For the Lord Most High is to be feared; he is the great King over all the earth.
47:3 He subdues the peoples under us, and the nations under our feet.
47:4 He chooses our inheritance for us, the pride of Jacob whom he loves.
47:5 God has gone up with a shout, the Lord with the sound of the ram's-horn.
47:6 Sing praises to God, sing praises; sing praises to our King, sing praises.
47:7 For God is the King of all the earth; sing praises with all your skill.
47:8 God reigns over the nations; God sits upon his holy throne.
47:9 The nobles of the peoples have gathered together with the people of the God of Abraham.
47:10 The shields of the earth belong to God; he is highly exalted.

text.NRSV:
48:1 Great is the Lord, and highly to be praised; in the city of our God is his holy hill.
48:2 Beautiful and lofty, the joy of all the earth, is the hill of Zion, the very center of the world and the city of the great King.
48:3 God is in her citadels; he is known to be her sure refuge.
48:4 Behold, the kings of the earth assembled and marched forward together.
48:5 They looked and were astounded; they retreated and fled in terror.
48:6 Trembling seized them there; they writhed like a woman in childbirth, like ships of the sea when the east wind shatters them.
48:7 As we have heard, so have we seen, in the city of the Lord of hosts, in the city of our God; God has established her for ever.
48:8 We have waited in silence on your loving-kindness, O God, in the midst of your temple.
48:9 As your Name, O God, so also your praise reaches to the ends of the earth.
48:10 Your right hand is full of justice; let Mount Zion be glad and the cities of Judah rejoice, because of your judgments.
48:11 Make the circuit of Zion; walk round about her; count the number of her towers.
48:12 Consider well her bulwarks; examine her strongholds; that you may tell those who come after.
48:13 This God is our God for ever and ever; he shall be our guide for evermore.

text.KJV:
48:1 Great is the LORD, and greatly to be praised in the city of our God, in the mountain of his holiness.
48:2 Beautiful for situation, the joy of the whole earth, is mount Zion, on the sides of the north, the city of the great King.
48:3 God is known in her palaces for a refuge.
48:4 For, lo, the kings were assembled, they passed by together.
48:5 They saw it, and so they marvelled; they were troubled, and hasted away.
48:6 Fear took hold upon them there, and pain, as of a woman in travail.
48:7 Thou breakest the ships of Tarshish with an east wind.
48:8 As we have heard, so have we seen in the city of the LORD of hosts, in the city of our God: God will establish it for ever. Selah.
48:9 We have thought of thy lovingkindness, O God, in the midst of thy temple.
48:10 According to thy name, O God, so is thy praise unto the ends of the earth: thy righteousness is full of right hand.
48:11 Let mount Zion rejoice, let the daughters of Judah be glad, because of thy judgments.
48:12 Walk about Zion, and go round about her: tell the towers thereof.
48:13 Mark ye well her bulwarks, consider her palaces; that ye may tell it to the generation following.
48:14 For this God is our God for ever and ever: he will be our guide even unto death.
text.JPS:
48:1 A song. A psalm of the sons of Korah.
48:2 Great is the LORD, and highly to be praised, in the city of our God, in His holy mountain.
48:3 Fair in situation, the joy of the whole earth, is mount Zion, on the sides of the north, the city of the great King.
48:4 God in her palaces has made Himself known for a high tower.
48:5 For, lo, the kings assembled themselves, they passed by together.
48:6 They saw it, then they were amazed; they were dismayed, they fled in haste.
48:7 Trembling took hold of them there, pain, as of a woman in travail.
48:8 With the east wind You break the ships of Tarshish.
48:9 As we have heard, so have we seen in the city of the LORD of hosts, in the city of our God; God will establish it forever. Selah.
48:10 We have thought on Your steadfast love, O God, in the midst of Your temple.
48:11 As is Your name, O God, so is Your praise unto the ends of the earth; Your right hand is full of righteousness.
48:12 Let mount Zion be glad, let the daughters of Judah rejoice, because of Your judgments.
48:13 Walk about Zion, and go round about her; count the towers thereof.
48:14 Mark well her bulwarks, consider her palaces; that you may tell it to the generation following.
48:15 For this God is our God forever and ever; He will be our guide even unto death.

text.NRSV:
49:1 Hear this, all you peoples; hearken, all you who dwell in the world,
49:2 You of high degree and low, rich and poor together.
49:3 My mouth shall speak of wisdom, and my heart shall meditate on understanding.
49:4 I will incline my ear to a proverb and set forth my riddle upon the harp.
49:5 Why should I be afraid in evil days, when the wickedness of those at my heels surrounds me,
49:6 The wickedness of those who put their trust in their goods, and boast of their great riches?
49:7 We can never ransom ourselves, or deliver to God the price of our life;
49:8 For the ransom of our life is so great, that we should never have enough to pay it,
49:9 In order to live for ever and ever, and never see the grave.
49:10 For we see that the wise die also; like the dull and stupid they perish and leave their wealth to those who come after them.
49:11 Their graves shall be their homes for ever, their dwelling places from generation to generation, though they call the lands after their own names.
49:12 Even though honored, they cannot live for ever; they are like the beasts that perish.
49:13 Such is the way of those who foolishly trust in themselves, and the end of those who delight in their own words.
49:14 Like a flock of sheep they are destined to die; Death is their shepherd; they go down straightway to the grave.
49:15 Their form shall waste away, and the land of the dead shall be their home.
49:16 But God will ransom my life; he will snatch me from the grasp of death.
49:17 Do not be envious when some become rich, or when the grandeur of their house increases;
49:18 For they will carry nothing away at their death, nor will their grandeur follow them.
49:19 Though they thought themselves fortunate in their lifetime, and men praise you when you do well for yourself,
49:20 They will join the company of their forebears, who will never see the light again.
49:21 Those who are honored, but have no understanding, are like the beasts that perish.
