text.KJV:
49:1 Hear this, all ye people; give ear, all ye inhabitants of the world:
49:2 Both low and high, rich and poor, together.
49:3 My mouth shall speak of wisdom; and the meditation of my heart shall be of understanding.
49:4 I will incline mine ear to a parable: I will open my dark saying upon the harp.
49:5 Wherefore should I fear in the days of evil, when the iniquity of my heels shall compass me about?
49:6 They that trust in their wealth, and boast themselves in the multitude of their riches;
49:7 None of them can by any means redeem his brother, nor give to God a ransom for him:
49:8 (For the redemption of their soul is precious, and it ceaseth for ever:)
49:9 That he should still live for ever, and not see corruption.
49:10 For he seeth that wise men die, likewise the fool and the brutish person perish, and leave their wealth to others.
49:11 Their inward thought is, that their houses shall continue for ever, and their dwelling places to all generations; they call their lands after their own names.
49:12 Nevertheless man being in honour abideth not: he is like the beasts that perish.
49:13 This their way is their folly: yet their posterity approve their sayings. Selah.
49:14 Like sheep they are laid in the grave; death shall feed on them; and the upright shall have dominion over them in the morning; and their beauty shall consume in the grave from their dwelling.
49:15 But God will redeem my soul from the power of the grave: for he shall receive me. Selah.
49:16 Be not thou afraid when one is made rich, when the glory of his house is increased;
49:17 For when he dieth he shall carry nothing away: his glory shall not descend after him.
49:18 Though while he lived he blessed his soul: and men will praise thee, when thou doest well to thyself.
49:19 He shall go to the generation of his fathers; they shall never see light.
49:20 Man that is in honour, and understandeth not, is like the beasts that perish.
text.JPS:
49:1 For the leader. Of the sons of Korah. A psalm.
49:2 Hear this, all you peoples; give ear, all you inhabitants of the world;
49:3 both low and high, rich and poor together.
49:4 My mouth shall speak wisdom, and the meditation of my heart shall be of understanding.
49:5 I will incline my ear to a parable; I will open my dark saying upon the lyre.
49:6 Wherefore should I fear in the days of evil, when the iniquity of my supplanters compasses me about,
49:7 of them that trust in their wealth, and boast themselves in the multitude of their riches?
49:8 No man can by any means redeem his brother, nor give to God a ransom for him—
49:9 for too costly is the redemption of their soul, and must be let alone forever—
49:10 that he should still live forever, that he should not see the Pit.
49:11 For he sees that wise men die, the fool and the brutish person perish together, and leave their wealth to others.
49:12 Their inward thought is, that their houses shall continue forever, and their dwelling-places to all generations; they call their lands after their own names.
49:13 But man abideth not in honor; he is like the beasts that perish.
49:14 This is the way of them that are foolish, and of those who after them approve their sayings. Selah.
49:15 Like sheep they are appointed for Sheol; Death shall be their shepherd; and the upright shall have dominion over them in the morning; and their form shall be for Sheol to consume, that there be no habitation for it.
49:16 But God will redeem my soul from the power of Sheol; for He shall receive me. Selah.
49:17 Be not afraid when a man waxeth rich, when the glory of his house is increased;
49:18 for when he dieth he shall carry nothing away; his glory shall not descend after him.
49:19 Though while he lived he blessed his soul—and men praise you when you do well to yourself—
49:20 it shall go to the generation of his fathers; they shall never see the light.
49:21 Man that is in honor, and understandeth not, is like the beasts that perish.

text.NRSV:
50:1 The Lord, the God of gods, has spoken; he has called the earth from the rising of the sun to its setting.
50:2 Out of Zion, perfect in its beauty, God reveals himself in glory.
50:3 Our God will come and will not keep silence; before him there is a consuming flame, and round about him a raging storm.
50:4 He calls the heavens and the earth from above to witness the judgment of his people.
50:5 'Gather before me my loyal followers, those who have made a covenant with me and sealed it with sacrifice.'
50:6 Let the heavens declare the rightness of his cause; for God himself is judge.
50:7 Hear, O my people, and I will speak: 'O Israel, I will bear witness against you; for I am God, your God.
50:8 I do not accuse you because of your sacrifices; your offerings are always before me.
50:9 I will take no bull-calf from your stalls, nor he-goats out of your pens;
50:10 For all the beasts of the forest are mine, the herds in their thousands upon the hills.
50:11 I know every bird in the sky, and the creatures of the fields are in my sight.
50:12 If I were hungry, I would not tell you, for the whole world is mine and all that is in it.
50:13 Do you think I eat the flesh of bulls, or drink the blood of goats?
50:14 Offer to God a sacrifice of thanksgiving and make good your vows to the Most High.
50:15 Call upon me in the day of trouble; I will deliver you, and you shall honor me.'
50:16 But to the wicked God says: 'Why do you recite my statutes, and take my covenant upon your lips;
50:17 Since you refuse discipline and toss my words behind your back?
50:18 When you see a thief, you make him your friend, and you cast in your lot with adulterers.
50:19 You have loosed your lips for evil and harnessed your tongue to a lie.
50:20 You are always speaking evil of your brother and slandering your own mother's son.
50:21 These things you have done, and I kept silent, and you thought that I am like you. I have made my accusation; I have put my case in order before your eyes.
50:22 Consider this well, you who forget God, lest I rend you and there be none to deliver you.
50:23 Whoever offers me the sacrifice of thanksgiving honors me; but to those who keep in my way will I show the salvation of God.'

text.KJV:
50:1 The mighty God, even the LORD, hath spoken, and called the earth from the rising of the sun unto the going down thereof.
50:2 Out of Zion, the perfection of beauty, God hath shined.
50:3 Our God shall come, and shall not keep silence: a fire shall devour before him, and it shall be very tempestuous round about him.
50:4 He shall call to the heavens from above, and to the earth, that he may judge his people.
50:5 Gather my saints together unto me; those that have made a covenant with me by sacrifice.
50:6 And the heavens shall declare his righteousness: for God is judge himself. Selah.
50:7 Hear, O my people, and I will speak; O Israel, and I will testify against thee: I am God, even thy God.
50:8 I will not reprove thee for thy sacrifices or thy burnt offerings, to have been continually before me.
50:9 I will take no bullock out of thy house, nor he goats out of thy folds.
50:10 For every beast of the forest is mine, and the cattle upon a thousand hills.
50:11 I know all the fowls of the mountains: and the wild beasts of the field are mine.
50:12 If I were hungry, I would not tell thee: for the world is mine, and the fulness thereof.
50:13 Will I eat the flesh of bulls, or drink the blood of goats?
50:14 Offer unto God thanksgiving; and pay thy vows unto the most High:
50:15 And call upon me in the day of trouble: I will deliver thee, and thou shalt glorify me.
50:16 But unto the wicked God saith, What hast thou to do to declare my statutes, or that thou shouldest take my covenant in thy mouth?
50:17 Seeing thou hatest instruction, and castest my words behind thee.
50:18 When thou sawest a thief, then thou consentedst with him, and hast been partaker with adulterers.
50:19 Thou givest thy mouth to evil, and thy tongue frameth deceit.
50:20 Thou sittest and speakest against thy brother; thou slanderest thine own mother's son.
50:21 These things hast thou done, and I kept silence; thou thoughtest that I was altogether such an one as thyself: but I will reprove thee, and set them in order before thine eyes.
50:22 Now consider this, ye that forget God, lest I tear you in pieces, and there be none to deliver.
50:23 Whoso offereth praise glorifieth me: and to him that ordereth his conversation aright will I shew the salvation of God.
text.JPS:
50:1 A psalm of Asaph. God, God, the LORD, has spoken, and called the earth from the rising of the sun unto the going down thereof.
50:2 Out of Zion, the perfection of beauty, God has shined forth.
50:3 Our God cometh, and doth not keep silence; a fire devoureth before Him, and round about Him it is very tempestuous.
50:4 He calleth to the heavens above, and to the earth, that He may judge His people:
50:5 "Gather My faithful ones together unto Me, those that have made a covenant with Me by sacrifice."
50:6 And the heavens declare His righteousness; for God Himself is judge. Selah.
50:7 "Hear, O My people, and I will speak; O Israel, and I will testify against you: God, your God, am I.
50:8 I will not reprove you for your sacrifices; and your burnt-offerings are continually before Me.
50:9 I will take no bullock out of your house, nor he-goats out of your folds.
50:10 For every beast of the forest is Mine, and the cattle upon a thousand hills.
50:11 I know all the fowls of the mountains; and the wild beasts of the field are Mine.
50:12 If I were hungry, I would not tell you; for the world is Mine, and the fullness thereof.
50:13 Will I eat the flesh of brawny bulls, or drink the blood of goats?
50:14 Offer unto God the sacrifice of thanksgiving, and pay your vows unto the Most High;
50:15 and call upon Me in the day of trouble; I will deliver you, and you shall glorify Me."
50:16 But unto the wicked God saith: "What hast you to do to declare My statutes, and that you hast taken My covenant in your mouth?
50:17 Seeing you hatest instruction, and castest My words behind you.
50:18 When you sawest a thief, you consentedst with him, and hast been partaker with adulterers.
50:19 You givest your mouth to evil, and your tongue framest deceit.
50:20 You sittest and speakest against your brother; you slanderest your own mother's son.
50:21 These things hast you done, and I kept silence; you thoughtest that I was altogether such a one as yourself; but I will reprove you, and set the cause before your eyes.
50:22 Now consider this, you that forget God, lest I tear you in pieces, and there be none to deliver.
50:23 Whoso offereth the sacrifice of thanksgiving glorifieth Me; and to him that ordereth his way aright will I show the salvation of God."

text.NRSV:
51:1 Have mercy on me, O God, according to your loving-kindness; in your great compassion blot out my offenses.
51:2 Wash me through and through from my wickedness and cleanse me from my sin.
51:3 For I know my transgressions, and my sin is ever before me.
51:4 Against you only have I sinned and done what is evil in your sight.
51:5 And so you are justified when you speak and upright in your judgment.
51:6 Indeed, I have been wicked from my birth, a sinner from my mother's womb.
51:7 For behold, you look for truth deep within me and will make me understand wisdom secretly.
51:8 Purge me from my sin, and I shall be pure; wash me, and I shall be whiter than snow.
51:9 Make me hear of joy and gladness, that the body you have broken may rejoice.
51:10 Hide your face from my sins and blot out all my iniquities.
51:11 Create in me a clean heart, O God, and renew a right spirit within me.
51:12 Cast me not away from your presence and take not your holy Spirit from me.
51:13 Give me the joy of your help again and sustain me with your willing Spirit.
51:14 I shall teach your ways to the wicked, and sinners shall return to you.
51:15 Deliver me from death, O God, and my tongue shall sing of your righteousness, O God of my salvation.
51:16 Open my lips, O Lord, and my mouth shall proclaim your praise.
51:17 Had you desired it, I would have offered sacrifice, but you take no delight in burnt-offerings.
51:18 The sacrifice of God is a troubled spirit; a broken and contrite heart, O God, you will not despise.
51:19 Be favorable and gracious to Zion, and rebuild the walls of Jerusalem.
51:20 Then you will be pleased with the appointed sacrifices, with burnt-offerings and oblations; then shall they offer young bullocks upon your altar.

51:16 Open my lips, O Lord, *
      and my mouth shall proclaim your praise.
51:17 Had you desired it, I would have offered sacrifice, *
      but you take no delight in burnt-offerings.
51:18 The sacrifice of God is a troubled spirit; *
      a broken and contrite heart, O God, you will not despise.
51:19 Be favorable and gracious to Zion, *
      and rebuild the walls of Jerusalem.
51:20 Then you will be pleased with the appointed sacrifices,
      with burnt-offerings and oblations; *
      then shall they offer young bullocks upon your altar.
text.KJV:
51:1 Have mercy upon me, O God, according to thy lovingkindness: according unto the multitude of thy tender mercies blot out my transgressions.
51:2 Wash me throughly from mine iniquity, and cleanse me from my sin.
51:3 For I acknowledge my transgressions: and my sin is ever before me.
51:4 Against thee, thee only, have I sinned, and done this evil in thy sight: that thou mightest be justified when thou speakest, and be clear when thou judgest.
51:5 Behold, I was shapen in iniquity; and in sin did my mother conceive me.
51:6 Behold, thou desirest truth in the inward parts: and in the hidden part thou shalt make me to know wisdom.
51:7 Purge me with hyssop, and I shall be clean: wash me, and I shall be whiter than snow.
51:8 Make me to hear joy and gladness; that the bones which thou hast broken may rejoice.
51:9 Hide thy face from my sins, and blot out all mine iniquities.
51:10 Create in me a clean heart, O God; and renew a right spirit within me.
51:11 Cast me not away from thy presence; and take not thy holy spirit from me.
51:12 Restore unto me the joy of thy salvation; and uphold me with thy free spirit.
51:13 Then will I teach transgressors thy ways; and sinners shall be converted unto thee.
51:14 Deliver me from bloodguiltiness, O God, thou God of my salvation: and my tongue shall sing aloud of thy righteousness.
51:15 O Lord, open thou my lips; and my mouth shall shew forth thy praise.
51:16 For thou desirest not sacrifice; else would I give it: thou delightest not in burnt offering.
51:17 The sacrifices of God are a broken spirit: a broken and a contrite heart, O God, thou wilt not despise.
51:18 Do good in thy good pleasure unto Zion: build thou the walls of Jerusalem.
51:19 Then shalt thou be pleased with the sacrifices of righteousness, with burnt offering and whole burnt offering: then shall they offer bullocks upon thine altar.
text.JPS:
51:1 For the leader. A psalm of David,
51:2 when Nathan the prophet came to him after he had gone to Bathsheba.
51:3 Have mercy on me, O God, as befits Your faithfulness; in Your great compassion blot out my transgressions.
51:4 Wash me thoroughly of my iniquity, and purify me of my sin;
51:5 for I recognize my transgressions, and my sin is ever before me.
51:6 Against You alone have I sinned, and done what is evil in Your sight; so You are just in Your sentence, and right in Your judgment.
51:7 Indeed I was born with iniquity; with sin my mother conceived me.
51:8 Indeed You desire truth about that which is hidden; teach me wisdom about secret things.
51:9 Purge me with hyssop till I am pure; wash me till I am whiter than snow.
51:10 Let me hear tidings of joy and gladness; let the bones You have crushed exult.
51:11 Hide Your face from my sins, and blot out all my iniquities.
51:12 Fashion a pure heart for me, O God; create in me a steadfast spirit.
51:13 Do not cast me out of Your presence, or take Your holy spirit from me.
51:14 Restore to me the joy of Your deliverance, and let a willing spirit sustain me.
51:15 I will teach transgressors Your ways, that sinners may return to You.
51:16 Save me from bloodguilt, O God, God, my deliverer, that my tongue may sing of Your beneficence.
51:17 O Lord, open my lips, and my mouth shall declare Your praise.
51:18 You do not desire sacrifice, else I would give it; a burnt-offering You do not want.
51:19 True sacrifice to God is a contrite spirit; God, You will not despise a contrite and crushed heart.
51:20 May it please You to make Zion prosper; rebuild the walls of Jerusalem.
51:21 Then You will desire sacrifices offered in righteousness, burnt-offering and whole-offering; then bulls will be offered on Your altar.

text.NRSV:
52:1 You tyrant, why do you boast of wickedness against the godly all day long?
52:2 You plot ruin; your tongue is like a sharpened razor, O worker of deception.
52:3 You love evil more than good and lying more than speaking the truth.
52:4 You love all words that hurt, O you deceitful tongue.
52:5 Oh, that God would demolish you utterly, topple you, and snatch you from your dwelling, and root you out of the land of the living!
52:6 The righteous shall see and tremble, and they shall laugh at him, saying,
52:7 'See the man who did not make God his stronghold, but trusted in the height of his wealth and relied upon his wickedness.'
52:8 But I am like a green olive tree in the house of God; I trust in the mercy of God for ever and ever.
52:9 I will give you thanks for ever for what you have done; in the presence of the faithful I will wait upon your Name, for it is good.
text.Coverdale:
52:1 You tyrant, why do you boast of wickedness *
      against the godly all day long?
52:2 You plot ruin;
      your tongue is like a sharpened razor, *
      O worker of deception.
52:3 You love evil more than good *
      and lying more than speaking the truth.
52:4 You love all words that hurt, *
      O you deceitful tongue.
52:5 Oh, that God would demolish you utterly, *
      topple you, and snatch you from your dwelling,
      and root you out of the land of the living!
52:6 The righteous shall see and tremble, *
      and they shall laugh at him, saying,
52:7 "This is the one who did not take God for a refuge, *
      but trusted in great wealth
      and relied upon wickedness."
52:8 But I am like a green olive tree in the house of God; *
      I trust in the mercy of God for ever and ever.
52:9 I will give you thanks for what you have done *
      and declare the goodness of your Name
      in the presence of the godly.
text.KJV:
52:1 Why boastest thou thyself in mischief, O mighty man? the goodness of God endureth continually.
52:2 Thy tongue deviseth mischiefs; like a sharp razor, working deceitfully.
52:3 Thou lovest evil more than good; and lying rather than to speak righteousness. Selah.
52:4 Thou lovest all devouring words, O thou deceitful tongue.
52:5 God shall likewise destroy thee for ever, he shall take thee away, and pluck thee out of thy dwelling place, and root thee out of the land of the living. Selah.
52:6 The righteous also shall see, and fear, and shall laugh at him:
52:7 Lo, this is the man that made not God his strength; but trusted in the abundance of his riches, and strengthened himself in his wickedness.
52:8 But I am like a green olive tree in the house of God: I trust in the mercy of God for ever and ever.
52:9 I will praise thee for ever, because thou hast done it: and I will wait on thy name; for it is good before thy saints.
text.JPS:
52:1 For the leader. A maskil of David,
52:2 when Doeg the Edomite came and told Saul, and said to him, "David has come to the house of Ahimelech."
52:3 Why do you boast of evil, brave man? God's faithfulness is unceasing.
52:4 Your tongue devises mischief, like a sharp razor that works deceit.
52:5 You love evil rather than good, falsehood rather than speaking what is right. Selah.
52:6 You love all destructive words, O deceitful tongue.
52:7 So God will tear you down forever; He will snatch you and pluck you from your tent, and uproot you from the land of the living. Selah.
52:8 The righteous will see it and be in awe; they will mock him, saying,
52:9 "Here is the man who did not make God his stronghold, but trusted in his great wealth, and relied upon his mischief."
52:10 But I am like a thriving olive tree in the house of God; I trust in the steadfast love of God forever and ever.
52:11 I will praise You forever for what You have done; I will look to Your name in the presence of Your faithful ones, for it is good.

text.NRSV:
53:1 The fool has said in his heart, 'There is no God.' They are corrupt, and have done abominable iniquity; there is none who does good.
53:2 God looks down from heaven upon the children of men, to see if there is any who is wise, who seeks after God.
53:3 Every one has gone astray; they have all become corrupt; there is none who does good, no, not one.
53:4 Have the workers of iniquity no knowledge, who eat up my people as they eat bread, and do not call upon God?
53:5 There they are, in great fear, where no fear was! For God has scattered the bones of those who encamp against you; you put them to shame, for God has rejected them.
53:6 Oh, that salvation for Israel would come out of Zion! When God restores the fortunes of his people, Jacob will rejoice; Israel will be glad.
text.Coverdale:
53:1 The fool has said in his heart, "There is no God." *
      All are corrupt and commit abominable acts;
      there is none who does any good.
53:2 God looks down from heaven upon us all, *
      to see if there is any who is wise,
      if there is one who seeks after God.
53:3 Every one has proved faithless;
      all alike have turned bad; *
      there is none who does good; no, not one.
53:4 Have they no knowledge, those evildoers *
      who eat up my people like bread
      and do not call upon God?
53:5 See how greatly they tremble,
      such trembling as never was; *
      for God has scattered the bones of the enemy;
      they are put to shame, because God has rejected them.
53:6 Oh, that Israel's deliverance would come out of Zion! *
      when God restores the fortunes of his people,
      Jacob will rejoice and Israel be glad.
text.KJV:
53:1 The fool hath said in his heart, There is no God. Corrupt are they, and have done abominable iniquity: there is none that doeth good.
53:2 God looked down from heaven upon the children of men, to see if there were any that did understand, that did seek God.
53:3 Every one of them is gone back: they are altogether become filthy; there is none that doeth good, no, not one.
53:4 Have the workers of iniquity no knowledge? who eat up my people as they eat bread: they have not called upon God.
53:5 There were they in great fear, where no fear was: for God hath scattered the bones of him that encampeth against thee: thou hast put them to shame, because God hath despised them.
53:6 Oh that the salvation of Israel were come out of Zion! When God bringeth back the captivity of his people, Jacob shall rejoice, and Israel shall be glad.
text.JPS:
53:1 For the leader; upon mahalath. A maskil of David.
53:2 The fool is convinced there is no God. They are corrupt, their deeds are vile; not one does good.
53:3 God looks down from heaven upon mankind to see if any are wise, if any seek God.
53:4 Everyone has turned turned away, all alike are tainted; not one does good, not even one.
53:5 Are they so ignorant, the evildoers, who devour my people as they devour bread, and do not invoke God?
53:6 There they will be seized with terror—such terror as never was!—for God will scatter the bones of your besiegers; you will put them to shame, for God has rejected them.
53:7 O that the deliverance of Israel might come from Zion! When God restores the fortunes of His people, Jacob will exult, Israel will rejoice.

text.NRSV:
54:1 Save me, O God, by your name, and vindicate me by your might.
54:2 Hear my prayer, O God; give ear to the words of my mouth.
54:3 For strangers have risen against me, and ruthless ones seek my life; they do not set God before their eyes. Selah
54:4 Behold, God is my helper; the Lord is the upholder of my life.
54:5 He will repay my enemies for their evil; in your faithfulness, put an end to them.
54:6 With a freewill offering I will sacrifice to you; I will give thanks to your name, O Lord, for it is good.
54:7 For he has delivered me from every trouble, and my eye has looked in triumph on my enemies.
text.Coverdale:
54:1 Save me, O God, by your Name; *
      in your might, defend my cause.
54:2 Hear my prayer, O God; *
      give ear to the words of my mouth.
54:3 For the arrogant have risen up against me,
      and the ruthless have sought my life, *
      those who have no regard for God.
54:4 Behold, God is my helper; *
      it is the Lord who sustains my life.
54:5 Render evil to those who spy on me; *
      in your faithfulness, destroy them.
54:6 I will offer you a freewill sacrifice *
      and praise your Name, O LORD, for it is good.
54:7 For you have rescued me from every trouble, *
      and my eye has seen the ruin of my foes.
text.KJV:
54:1 Save me, O God, by thy name, and judge me by thy strength.
54:2 Hear my prayer, O God; give ear to the words of my mouth.
54:3 For strangers are risen up against me, and oppressors seek after my soul: they have not set God before them. Selah.
54:4 Behold, God is mine helper: the Lord is with them that uphold my soul.
54:5 He shall reward evil unto mine enemies: cut them off in thy truth.
54:6 I will freely sacrifice unto thee: I will praise thy name, O LORD; for it is good.
54:7 For he hath delivered me out of all trouble: and mine eye hath seen his desire upon mine enemies.
text.JPS:
54:1 For the leader; with instrumental music. A maskil of David,
54:2 when the Ziphites came and said to Saul, "Know, David is hiding among us."
54:3 O God, deliver me by Your name; by Your power vindicate me.
54:4 O God, hear my prayer; give ear to the words of my mouth.
54:5 For strangers have risen up against me, and ruthless men seek my life; they are not mindful of God. Selah.
54:6 See, God is my helper; the Lord is the support of my life.
54:7 He will requite my watchful foes with evil; in Your faithfulness destroy them.
54:8 Out of a willing heart I will offer sacrifice to You; I will praise Your name, O LORD, for it is good,
54:9 for it has rescued me from every trouble, and my eye has looked on my enemies.

text.NRSV:
55:1 Give ear to my prayer, O God, and do not hide yourself from my supplication.
55:2 Attend to me, and answer me; I am restless in my complaint and I moan,
55:3 because of the noise of the enemy, because of the oppression of the wicked. For they drop trouble upon me, and in anger they bear a grudge against me.
55:4 My heart is in anguish within me, the terrors of death have fallen upon me.
55:5 Fear and trembling come upon me, and horror overwhelms me.
55:6 And I say, 'O that I had wings like a dove! I would fly away and be at rest;'
55:7 truly, I would flee far away; I would lodge in the wilderness;
55:8 I would hurry to find a shelter for myself from the raging wind and tempest.'
55:9 Confuse, O Lord, confound their speech; for I see violence and strife in the city.
55:10 Day and night they go around it on its walls, and iniquity and trouble are within it;
55:11 ruin is in its midst; oppression and fraud do not depart from its marketplace.
55:12 It is not enemies who taunt me—I could bear that; it is not adversaries who deal insolently with me—I could hide from them.
55:13 But it is you, my equal, my companion, my familiar friend,
55:14 with whom I kept pleasant company; we walked in the house of God with the throng.
55:15 Let death come upon them; let them go down alive to Sheol; for evil is in their homes and in their hearts.
text.Coverdale:
55:1 Hear my prayer, O God; *
      do not hide yourself from my petition.
55:2 Listen to me and answer me; *
      I have no peace, because of my cares.
55:3 I am shaken by the noise of the enemy *
      and by the pressure of the wicked;
55:4 For they have cast trouble upon me, *
      and in their anger they bear a grudge against me.
55:5 My heart quakes within me, *
      and the terrors of death have fallen upon me.
55:6 Fear and trembling have come over me, *
      and horror overwhelms me.
55:7 And I said, "Oh, that I had wings like a dove! *
      I would fly away and be at rest.
55:8 I would flee to a far-off place *
      and make my lodging in the wilderness.
55:9 I would hasten to escape *
      from the stormy wind and tempest."
55:10 Swallow them up, O Lord;
      confound their speech; *
      for I have seen violence and strife in the city.
55:11 Day and night the watchmen make their rounds upon her walls, *
      but trouble and misery are in the midst of her.
55:12 There is corruption at her heart; *
      her streets are never free of oppression and deceit.
55:13 For had it been an adversary who taunted me,
      then I could have borne it; *
      or had it been an enemy who vaunted himself against me,
      then I could have hidden from him.
55:14 But it was you, a man after my own heart, *
      my companion, my own familiar friend.
55:15 We took sweet counsel together, *
      and walked with the throng in the house of God.
55:16 Let death come upon them suddenly;
      let them go down alive to the grave; *
      for wickedness is in their dwellings, in their very midst.
55:17 But I will call upon God, *
      and the LORD will deliver me.
55:18 In the evening, in the morning, and at the noonday,
      I will complain and lament, *
      and he will hear my voice.
55:19 He will bring me safely back from the battle waged against me; *
      for there are many who fight me.
55:20 God, who is enthroned of old, will hear me and bring them down; *
      they never change; they do not fear God.
55:21 My companion stretched forth his hand against his comrade; *
      he has broken his covenant.
55:22 His speech is softer than butter, *
      but war is in his heart.
55:23 His words are smoother than oil, *
      but they are drawn swords.
55:24 Cast your burden upon the LORD,
      and he will sustain you; *
      he will never let the righteous stumble.
55:25 For you will bring the bloodthirsty and deceitful *
      down to the pit of destruction, O God.
55:26 They shall not live out half their days, *
      but I will put my trust in you.
text.KJV:
55:1 Give ear to my prayer, O God; and hide not thyself from my supplication.
55:2 Attend unto me, and hear me: I mourn in my complaint, and make a noise;
55:3 Because of the voice of the enemy, because of the oppression of the wicked: for they cast iniquity upon me, and in wrath they hate me.
55:4 My heart is sore pained within me: and the terrors of death are fallen upon me.
55:5 Fearfulness and trembling are come upon me, and horror hath overwhelmed me.
55:6 And I said, Oh that I had wings like a dove! for then would I fly away, and be at rest.
55:7 Lo, then would I wander far off, and remain in the wilderness. Selah.
55:8 I would hasten my escape from the windy storm and tempest.
55:9 Destroy, O Lord, and divide their tongues: for I have seen violence and strife in the city.
55:10 Day and night they go about it upon the walls thereof: mischief also and sorrow are in the midst of it.
55:11 Wickedness is in the midst thereof: deceit and guile depart not from her streets.
55:12 For it was not an enemy that reproached me; then I could have borne it: neither was it he that hated me that did magnify himself against me; then I would have hid myself from him:
55:13 But it was thou, a man mine equal, my guide, and mine acquaintance.
55:14 We took sweet counsel together, and walked unto the house of God in company.
55:15 Let death seize upon them, and let them go down quick into hell: for wickedness is in their dwellings, and among them.
55:16 As for me, I will call upon God; and the LORD shall save me.
55:17 Evening, and morning, and at noon, will I pray, and cry aloud: and he shall hear my voice.
55:18 He hath delivered my soul in peace from the battle that was against me: for there were many with me.
55:19 God shall hear, and afflict them, even he that abideth of old. Selah. Because they have no changes, therefore they fear not God.
55:20 He hath put forth his hands against such as be at peace with him: he hath broken his covenant.
55:21 The words of his mouth were smoother than butter, but war was in his heart: his words were softer than oil, yet were they drawn swords.
55:22 Cast thy burden upon the LORD, and he shall sustain thee: he shall never suffer the righteous to be moved.
55:23 But thou, O God, shalt bring them down into the pit of destruction: bloody and deceitful men shall not live out half their days; but I will trust in thee.
text.JPS:
55:1 For the leader; with instrumental music. A maskil of David.
55:2 Give ear, O God, to my prayer; do not ignore my supplication.
55:3 Pay heed to me and answer me. I am tossed about as I complain; I moan
55:4 at the voice of the enemy, under the oppression of the wicked; for they bring down trouble upon me, and in wrath harass me.
55:5 My heart flutters within me; the terrors of Death have fallen upon me.
55:6 Fear and trembling beset me; horror overwhelms me.
55:7 And I say, "O that I had wings like a dove! I would fly away and find rest.
55:8 I would flee far away; I would lodge in the wilderness. Selah.
55:9 I would hasten to find shelter for myself from the sweeping wind, from the tempest."
55:10 Confuse them, O Lord; confound their speech! For I see lawlessness and strife in the city;
55:11 day and night they go around it upon its walls; mischief and trouble are within it;
55:12 malice is within it; oppression and deceit do not leave its marketplace.
55:13 For it is not an enemy who reviles me—that I could bear; it is not my foe who vaunts himself against me—from him I could hide;
55:14 but it is you, a man my equal, my companion, my close friend;
55:15 we who enjoyed sweet companionship together, walked in procession in the house of God.
55:16 May death find them out; let them go down alive to Sheol; for evil is in their homes and in their hearts.
55:17 As for me, I call upon God, and the LORD will deliver me.
55:18 Evening, morning, and noon I complain and moan, and He hears my voice.
55:19 He redeems my life in safety from the battle against me, for there are many against me.
55:20 God will hear and answer them—He who is enthroned from of old, Selah—those who do not change, and do not fear God.
55:21 He has laid hands on his allies; he has violated his covenant.
55:22 His talk is smoother than butter, but war is in his heart; his words are softer than oil, yet they are drawn swords.
55:23 Cast your burden upon the LORD, and He will sustain you; He will never let the righteous man falter.
55:24 For You, O God, will bring them down to the nethermost Pit; bloodthirsty and deceitful men shall not live out half their days. But I trust in You.

text.NRSV:
56:1 Have mercy on me, O God, for my enemies are hounding me; all day long they assault and oppress me.
56:2 My adversaries hound me all day long, and many are they who fight against me, O Most High.
56:3 Whenever I am afraid, I will put my trust in you.
56:4 In God, whose word I praise, in God I trust and will not be afraid, for what can flesh do to me?
56:5 All day long they damage my cause; their only thought is to do me evil.
56:6 They band together and lie in wait; they spy upon my footsteps; because they seek my life.
56:7 Shall they escape despite their wickedness? O God, in your anger, cast down the peoples.
56:8 You have noted my lamentation; put my tears into your bottle; are they not recorded in your book?
56:9 Whenever I call upon you, my enemies will be put to flight; this I know, for God is on my side.
56:10 In God the Lord, whose word I praise, in God I trust and will not be afraid, for what can mortals do to me?
56:11 I am bound by the vows I made to you, O God; I will present to you thank-offerings.
56:12 For you have rescued my soul from death and my feet from stumbling, that I may walk before God in the light of the living.
text.Coverdale:
56:1 Have mercy on me, O God,
      for my enemies are hounding me; *
      all day long they assault and oppress me.
56:2 My foes are hounding me all day long; *
      truly there are many who fight against me, O Most High.
56:3 When I am afraid, *
      I put my trust in you.
56:4 In God, whose word I praise,
      in God I trust and will not be afraid, *
      for what can flesh do to me?
56:5 All day long they damage my cause; *
      their only thought is to do me evil.
56:6 They band together; they lie in wait; *
      they spy upon my footsteps;
      because they seek my life.
56:7 Shall they escape despite their wickedness? *
      O God, in your anger, cast down the peoples.
56:8 You have noted my lamentation;
      put my tears in your bottle; *
      are they not recorded in your book?
56:9 Whenever I call upon you, my enemies will be put to flight; *
      this I know, for God is on my side.
56:10 In God the LORD, whose word I praise,
      in God I trust and will not be afraid, *
      for what can mortals do to me?
56:11 I am bound by the vow I made to you, O God; *
      I will present my thank-offerings to you.
56:12 For you have rescued my soul from death and my feet from stumbling, *
      that I may walk before God in the light of the living.
text.KJV:
56:1 Be merciful unto me, O God: for man would swallow me up; he fighting daily oppresseth me.
56:2 Mine enemies would daily swallow me up: for they be many that fight against me, O thou most High.
56:3 What time I am afraid, I will trust in thee.
56:4 In God I will praise his word, in God I have put my trust; I will not fear what flesh can do unto me.
56:5 Every day they wrest my words: all their thoughts are against me for evil.
56:6 They gather themselves together, they hide themselves, they mark my steps, when they wait for my soul.
56:7 Shall they escape by iniquity? in thine anger cast down the people, O God.
56:8 Thou tellest my wanderings: put thou my tears into thy bottle: are they not in thy book?
56:9 When I cry unto thee, then shall mine enemies turn back: this I know; for God is for me.
56:10 In God will I praise his word: in the LORD will I praise his word.
56:11 In God have I put my trust: I will not be afraid what man can do unto me.
56:12 Thy vows are upon me, O God: I will render praises unto thee.
56:13 For thou hast delivered my soul from death: wilt not thou deliver my feet from falling, that I may walk before God in the light of the living?
text.JPS:
56:1 For the leader; upon jonath-elem-rehokim. Of David. A michtam; when the Philistines seized him in Gath.
56:2 Have mercy on me, O God, for man pants to devour me; all day long the warrior oppresses me.
56:3 My watchful foes pant to devour me all day long; for many are they who fight against me arrogantly.
56:4 On the day I am afraid, I trust in You.
56:5 In God, whose word I praise, in God I trust; I am not afraid; what can flesh do to me?
56:6 All day long they twist my words; all their thoughts are against me for evil.
56:7 They band together, they lurk, they watch my steps, as they look to take my life.
56:8 Pay them back for their mischief; in Your anger bring down the peoples, O God.
56:9 You keep count of my flitting; put my tears into Your bottle—are they not in Your book?
56:10 Then shall my enemies turn back on the day I call; this I know, that God is for me.
56:11 In God, whose word I praise, in the LORD, whose word I praise,
56:12 in God I trust; I am not afraid; what can man do to me?
56:13 Your vows are upon me, O God; I will render thank-offerings to You.
56:14 For You have rescued my life from death, my feet from stumbling, that I may walk before God in the light of the living.

text.NRSV:
57:1 Be merciful to me, O God, be merciful, for I have taken refuge in you; in the shadow of your wings will I take refuge until this time of trouble has passed.
57:2 I will call upon the Most High God, the God who maintains my cause.
57:3 He will send from heaven and save me; he will confound those who trample upon me; God will send forth his love and his faithfulness.
57:4 I lie in the midst of lions that devour the people; their teeth are spears and arrows, their tongue a sharp sword.
57:5 Be exalted, O God, above the heavens; let your glory be over all the earth.
57:6 They have laid a net for my feet, and I am bowed low; they have dug a pit before me, but have fallen into it themselves.
57:7 My heart is firmly fixed, O God, my heart is fixed; I will sing and make melody.
57:8 Wake up, my spirit; awake, lute and harp; I myself will waken the dawn.
57:9 I will confess you among the peoples, O Lord; I will sing praise to you among the nations.
57:10 For your loving-kindness is greater than the heavens, and your faithfulness reaches to the clouds.
57:11 Be exalted, O God, above the heavens; let your glory be over all the earth.
text.Coverdale:
57:1 Be merciful to me, O God, be merciful,
      for I have taken refuge in you; *
      in the shadow of your wings will I take refuge
      until this time of trouble has gone by.
57:2 I will call upon the Most High God, *
      the God who maintains my cause.
57:3 He will send from heaven and save me;
      he will confound those who trample upon me; *
      God will send forth his love and his faithfulness.
57:4 I lie in the midst of lions that devour the people; *
      their teeth are spears and arrows,
      their tongue a sharp sword.
57:5 They have laid a net for my feet,
      and I am bowed low; *
      they have dug a pit before me,
      but have fallen into it themselves.
57:6 Exalt yourself above the heavens, O God, *
      and your glory over all the earth.
57:7 My heart is firmly fixed, O God, my heart is fixed; *
      I will sing and make melody.
57:8 Wake up, my spirit;
      awake, lute and harp; *
      I myself will waken the dawn.
57:9 I will confess you among the peoples, O LORD; *
      I will sing praise to you among the nations.
57:10 For your loving-kindness is greater than the heavens, *
      and your faithfulness reaches to the clouds.
57:11 Exalt yourself above the heavens, O God, *
      and your glory over all the earth.
text.KJV:
57:1 Be merciful unto me, O God, be merciful unto me: for my soul trusteth in thee: yea, in the shadow of thy wings will I make my refuge, until these calamities be overpast.
57:2 I will cry unto God most high; unto God that performeth all things for me.
57:3 He shall send from heaven, and save me from the reproach of him that would swallow me up. Selah. God shall send forth his mercy and his truth.
57:4 My soul is among lions: and I lie even among them that are set on fire, even the sons of men, whose teeth are spears and arrows, and their tongue a sharp sword.
57:5 Be thou exalted, O God, above the heavens; let thy glory be above all the earth.
57:6 They have prepared a net for my steps; my soul is bowed down: they have digged a pit before me, into the midst whereof they are fallen themselves. Selah.
57:7 My heart is fixed, O God, my heart is fixed: I will sing and give praise.
57:8 Awake up, my glory; awake, psaltery and harp: I myself will awake early.
57:9 I will praise thee, O Lord, among the people: I will sing unto thee among the nations.
57:10 For thy mercy is great unto the heavens, and thy truth unto the clouds.
57:11 Be thou exalted, O God, above the heavens: let thy glory be above all the earth.
text.JPS:
57:1 For the leader; al-tashheth. Of David. A michtam; when he fled from Saul into the cave.
57:2 Have mercy on me, O God, have mercy on me, for in You my soul takes refuge; in the shadow of Your wings I take refuge till the destruction passes.
57:3 I call upon God Most High, upon God who fulfills His purpose for me.
57:4 He will send from heaven and deliver me, exposing him who pants to devour me, Selah; God will send out His steadfast love and His truth.
57:5 My soul is among lions; I lie among those who blaze, mortal men whose teeth are spears and arrows, and their tongue a sharp sword.
57:6 Be exalted, O God, above the heavens; let Your glory be over all the earth.
57:7 They prepared a net for my steps; my soul was bowed down; they dug a pit before me, and fell into it themselves. Selah.
57:8 My heart is steadfast, O God, my heart is steadfast; I will sing and hymn.
57:9 Awake, my glory! Awake, harp and lyre! I will wake the dawn.
57:10 I will praise You among the peoples, O Lord; I will hymn You among the nations.
57:11 For Your steadfast love is great unto the heavens, and Your truth unto the skies.
57:12 Be exalted, O God, above the heavens; let Your glory be over all the earth.

text.NRSV:
58:1 Do you indeed speak justly in council? do you judge with equity, you mortals?
58:2 No, in your mind you devise wickedness, and your hands deal out violence in the land.
58:3 The wicked are estranged from the womb; they have gone astray from their birth and speak lies.
58:4 Their poison is like the poison of a serpent; they are like the deaf adder that stops its ears,
58:5 Which does not heed the voice of the charmer, no matter how skillful his charming.
58:6 O God, break their teeth in their mouths; pull the fangs of the young lions, O Lord.
58:7 Let them vanish like water that runs off; let them wither like trodden grass.
58:8 Let them be like the snail that melts away, like a dead-born child that never sees the sun.
58:9 Before they bear fruit, let them be cut down like a brier; let them be swept away like the thorns of a desert.
58:10 The righteous shall rejoice when they see the vengeance; they shall wash their feet in the blood of the wicked.
58:11 And people shall say, 'Surely there is a reward for the righteous; surely there is a God who rules in the earth.'
text.Coverdale:
58:1 Do you indeed decree righteousness, you rulers? *
      do you judge the peoples with equity?
58:2 No; you devise evil in your hearts, *
      and your hands deal out violence in the land.
58:3 The wicked are perverse from the womb; *
      liars go astray from their birth.
58:4 They are as venomous as a serpent, *
      they are like the deaf adder which stops its ears,
58:5 Which does not heed the voice of the charmer, *
      no matter how skillful his charming.
58:6 O God, break their teeth in their mouths; *
      pull the fangs of the young lions, O LORD.
58:7 Let them vanish like water that runs off; *
      let them wither like trodden grass.
58:8 Let them be like the snail that melts away, *
      like a stillborn child that never sees the sun.
58:9 Before they bear fruit, let them be cut down like a brier; *
      like thorns and thistles let them be swept away.
58:10 The righteous will be glad when they see the vengeance; *
      they will bathe their feet in the blood of the wicked.
58:11 And people will say,
      "Surely, there is a reward for the righteous; *
      surely, there is a God who rules in the earth."
text.KJV:
58:1 Do ye indeed speak righteousness, O congregation? do ye judge uprightly, O ye sons of men?
58:2 Yea, in heart ye work wickedness; ye weigh the violence of your hands in the earth.
58:3 The wicked are estranged from the womb: they go astray as soon as they be born, speaking lies.
58:4 Their poison is like the poison of a serpent: they are like the deaf adder that stoppeth her ear;
58:5 Which will not hearken to the voice of charmers, charming never so wisely.
58:6 Break their teeth, O God, in their mouth: break out the great teeth of the young lions, O LORD.
58:7 Let them melt away as waters which run continually: when he bendeth his bow to shoot his arrows, let them be as cut in pieces.
58:8 As a snail which melteth, let every one of them pass away: like the untimely birth of a woman, that they may not see the sun.
58:9 Before your pots can feel the thorns, he shall take them away as with a whirlwind, both living, and in his wrath.
58:10 The righteous shall rejoice when he seeth the vengeance: he shall wash his feet in the blood of the wicked.
58:11 So that a man shall say, Verily there is a reward for the righteous: verily he is a God that judgeth in the earth.
text.JPS:
58:1 For the leader; al-tashheth. Of David. A michtam.
58:2 Do you indeed speak righteousness, O divine beings? Do you judge mankind equitably?
58:3 Nay, in your heart you devise lawlessness; in the land your hands weigh out violence.
58:4 The wicked go astray from the womb; the speakers of lies err from birth.
58:5 Their venom is like the venom of a snake; they are like a deaf asp that stops its ear,
58:6 so that it hears not the voice of charmers, or of the cunning weaver of spells.
58:7 O God, break the teeth in their mouths; shatter the jaws of the lions, O LORD.
58:8 Let them melt away like water that flows apace; when he aims his arrows, let them be as if headless.
58:9 Let them be like a snail that melts as it crawls, like an abortion that never saw the sun.
58:10 Before your pots can feel the thorns, whether green or burning, He will sweep it away.
58:11 The righteous man will rejoice when he sees vengeance; he will wash his feet in the blood of the wicked.
58:12 And men will say, "Truly there is a reward for the righteous; truly there is a God who judges the earth."

text.NRSV:
59:1 Deliver me from my enemies, O my God; protect me from those who rise up against me.
59:2 Deliver me from those who work evil; from the bloodthirsty save me.
59:3 Even now they lie in wait for my life; the mighty stir up strife against me. For no transgression or sin of mine, O Lord,
59:4 for no fault of mine, they run and make ready. Rouse yourself, come to my help and see!
59:5 You, Lord God of hosts, are God of Israel. Awake to punish all the nations; spare none of those who treacherously plot evil. Selah
59:6 They come each evening howling like dogs and prowl about the city.
59:7 See, they spew with their mouths, with swords in their lips—for 'Who,' they think, 'will hear us?'
59:8 But you, O Lord, laugh at them; you hold all the nations in derision.
59:9 O my strength, I will watch for you; for you, O God, are my fortress.
59:10 My God in his steadfast love will meet me; my God will let me look in triumph on my enemies.
59:11 Do not kill them, lest my people forget; scatter them by your power, and bring them down, O Lord, our shield.
59:12 For the sin of their mouths, the words of their lips, let them be trapped in their pride. For the cursing and lies that they utter,
59:13 consume them in wrath; consume them until they are no more, so that they may know that God rules over Jacob to the ends of the earth. Selah
59:14 Each evening they come howling like dogs and prowl about the city.
59:15 They roam about for food, and growl if they do not get their fill.
59:16 But I will sing of your might; I will sing aloud of your steadfast love in the morning. For you have been a fortress for me and a refuge in the day of my distress.
59:17 O my strength, I will sing praises to you, for you, O God, are my fortress, the God who shows me steadfast love.
text.Coverdale:
59:1 Rescue me from my enemies, O God; *
      protect me from those who rise up against me.
59:2 Rescue me from evildoers *
      and save me from those who thirst for my blood.
59:3 See how they lie in wait for my life,
      how the mighty gather together against me; *
      not for any offense or fault of mine, O LORD.
59:4 Not because of any guilt of mine
      they run and prepare themselves for battle. *
      Rouse yourself, come to my side, and see;
59:5 For you, LORD God of hosts, are Israel's God;
      awake to punish all the ungodly; *
      show no mercy to those who treacherously plot evil.
59:6 They go to and fro in the evening; *
      they snarl like dogs and run about the city.
59:7 Behold, they belch out with their mouths,
      and swords are on their lips; *
      for who, they say, will hear us?
59:8 But you, O LORD, you laugh at them; *
      you laugh all the ungodly to scorn.
59:9 My strong one, I will keep watch for you; *
      for you, O God, are my stronghold.
59:10 My merciful God comes to meet me; *
      God will let me look in triumph on my enemies.
59:11 Slay them, O God, lest my people forget; *
      send them reeling by your might
      and put them down, O Lord our shield.
59:12 For the sins of their mouth, for the words of their lips,
      for the cursing and lies that they utter, *
      let them be caught in their pride.
59:13 Make an end of them in your wrath;
      make an end of them, and they shall be no more; *
      let them know that God rules in Jacob,
      and to the ends of the earth.
59:14 They go to and fro in the evening; *
      they snarl like dogs and run about the city.
59:15 They forage for food, *
      and if they are not filled, they howl.
59:16 But I will sing of your strength *
      and celebrate your mercy in the morning;
59:17 For you have been my stronghold *
      and a refuge in the day of my trouble.
59:18 To you, O my strength, will I sing; *
      for you, O God, are my stronghold and my merciful God.
text.KJV:
59:1 Deliver me from mine enemies, O my God: defend me from them that rise up against me.
59:2 Deliver me from the workers of iniquity, and save me from bloody men.
59:3 For, lo, they lie in wait for my soul: the mighty are gathered against me; not for my transgression, nor for my sin, O LORD.
59:4 They run and prepare themselves without my fault: awake to help me, and behold.
59:5 Thou therefore, O LORD God of hosts, the God of Israel, awake to visit all the heathen: be not merciful to any wicked transgressors. Selah.
59:6 They return at evening: they make a noise like a dog, and go round about the city.
59:7 Behold, they belch out with their mouth: swords are in their lips: for who, say they, doth hear?
59:8 But thou, O LORD, shalt laugh at them; thou shalt have all the heathen in derision.
59:9 Because of his strength will I wait upon thee: for God is my defence.
59:10 The God of my mercy shall prevent me: God shall let me see my desire upon mine enemies.
59:11 Slay them not, lest my people forget: scatter them by thy power; and bring them down, O Lord our shield.
59:12 For the sin of their mouth and the words of their lips let them even be taken in their pride: and for cursing and lying which they speak.
59:13 Consume them in wrath, consume them, that they may not be: and let them know that God ruleeth in Jacob unto the ends of the earth. Selah.
59:14 And at evening let them return; and let them make a noise like a dog, and go round about the city.
59:15 Let them wander up and down for meat, and grudge if they be not satisfied.
59:16 But I will sing of thy power; yea, I will sing aloud of thy mercy in the morning: for thou hast been my defence and refuge in the day of my trouble.
59:17 Unto thee, O my strength, will I sing: for God is my defence, and the God of my mercy.
text.JPS:
59:1 For the leader; al-tashheth. Of David. A michtam; when Saul sent, and they watched the house to kill him.
59:2 Deliver me from my enemies, O my God; protect me from those who rise up against me.
59:3 Deliver me from evildoers, and save me from bloodthirsty men.
59:4 For see, they lurk for my life; fierce men band together against me, for no transgression or sin of mine, O LORD.
59:5 For no fault of mine they run and prepare themselves; awake to help me, and look.
59:6 You, LORD God of hosts, the God of Israel, rouse Yourself to punish all the nations; spare none of those who treacherously plot evil. Selah.
59:7 They return at evening, they howl like dogs, and go around the city.
59:8 See, they belch out with their mouth; swords are in their lips: "For who," they think, "hears?"
59:9 But You, O LORD, laugh at them; You mock all the nations.
59:10 O my strength, I look to You; for God is my stronghold.
59:11 My God in His steadfast love will meet me; God will let me look upon my watchful foes.
59:12 Do not destroy them, lest my people forget; scatter them by Your power, and bring them down, O Lord, our shield.
59:13 For the sin of their mouth, the word of their lips, let them be trapped in their pride, and for the cursing and lies they utter.
59:14 Consume them in wrath, consume them till they are no more; that they may know that God rules over Jacob, unto the ends of the earth. Selah.
59:15 They return at evening, they howl like dogs, and go around the city.
59:16 They wander about for food, and growl if they are not sated.
59:17 But I will sing of Your strength; I will sing aloud of Your steadfast love in the morning; for You have been a stronghold for me, a refuge on the day of my distress.
59:18 O my strength, I will hymn You; for God is my stronghold, the God of my steadfast love.

text.NRSV:
60:1 O God, you have rejected us, broken our defenses; you have been angry; now restore us!
60:2 You have caused the land to quake; you have torn it open; repair the cracks in it, for it is tottering.
60:3 You have made your people suffer hard things; you have given us wine to drink that makes us reel.
60:4 You have set up a banner for those who fear you, to rally to it from the bow. Selah
60:5 Give victory with your right hand, and answer us, so that those whom you love may be rescued.
60:6 God has spoken in his sanctuary: 'With exultation I will divide up Shechem, and portion out the Vale of Succoth.
60:7 Gilead is mine, and Manasseh is mine; Ephraim is my helmet; Judah is my scepter.
60:8 Moab is my washbasin; on Edom I hurl my shoe; over Philistia I shout in triumph.'
60:9 Who will bring me to the fortified city? Who will lead me to Edom?
60:10 Have you not rejected us, O God? You do not go out, O God, with our armies.
60:11 O grant us help against the foe, for human help is worthless.
60:12 With God we shall do valiantly; it is he who will tread down our foes.
text.Coverdale:
60:1 O God, you have cast us off and broken us; *
      you have been angry;
      oh, restore us to yourself again.
60:2 You have shaken the earth and split it open; *
      repair the cracks in it, for it totters.
60:3 You have made your people know hardship; *
      you have given us wine that makes us stagger.
60:4 You have set up a banner for those who fear you, *
      to be a refuge from the power of the bow.
60:5 Save us by your right hand and answer us, *
      that those whom you love may be delivered.
60:6 God spoke from his holy place and said: *
      "I will exult and parcel out Shechem;
      I will divide the valley of Succoth.
60:7 Gilead is mine and Manasseh is mine; *
      Ephraim is my helmet and Judah my scepter.
60:8 Moab is my wash-basin,
      on Edom I throw down my sandal to claim it, *
      and over Philistia will I shout in triumph."
60:9 Who will lead me into the strong city? *
      who will bring me into Edom?
60:10 Have you not cast us off, O God? *
      you no longer go out, O God, with our armies.
60:11 Grant us your help against the enemy, *
      for vain is the help of man.
60:12 With God we will do valiant deeds, *
      and he shall tread our enemies under foot.
text.KJV:
60:1 O God, thou hast cast us off, thou hast scattered us, thou hast been displeased; O turn thyself to us again.
60:2 Thou hast made the earth to tremble; thou hast broken it: heal the breaches thereof; for it shaketh.
60:3 Thou hast shewed thy people hard things: thou hast made us to drink the wine of astonishment.
60:4 Thou hast given a banner to them that fear thee, that it may be displayed because of the truth. Selah.
60:5 That thy beloved may be delivered; save with thy right hand, and hear me.
60:6 God hath spoken in his holiness; I will rejoice, I will divide Shechem, and mete out the valley of Succoth.
60:7 Gilead is mine, and Manasseh is mine; Ephraim also is the strength of mine head; Judah is my lawgiver;
60:8 Moab is my washpot; over Edom will I cast out my shoe: Philistia, triumph thou because of me.
60:9 Who will bring me into the strong city? who will lead me into Edom?
60:10 Wilt not thou, O God, which hadst cast us off? and thou, O God, which didst not go out with our armies?
60:11 Give us help from trouble: for vain is the help of man.
60:12 Through God we shall do valiantly: for he it is that shall tread down our enemies.
text.JPS:
60:1 For the leader; upon Shushan Eduth. A michtam of David, to teach;
60:2 when he strove with Aram-naharaim and with Aram-zobah, and Joab returned, and smote of Edom in the Valley of Salt twelve thousand.
60:3 O God, You have cast us off, You have broken us; You have been angry; restore us.
60:4 You have made the land quake, You have torn it open; heal its breaches, for it totters.
60:5 You have made Your people see hard things; You have given us wine of staggering to drink.
60:6 You have given a banner to those who fear You, to be displayed because of the truth. Selah.
60:7 That Your beloved ones may be delivered, save with Your right hand, and answer me.
60:8 God has spoken in His holiness: "I will exult; I will divide Shechem, and mete out the valley of Succoth.
60:9 Gilead is Mine, and Manasseh is Mine; Ephraim also is the defense of My head; Judah is My scepter.
60:10 Moab is My washpot; upon Edom I cast My shoe; Philistia, shout because of Me!"
60:11 Who will bring me into the fortified city? Who will lead me unto Edom?
60:12 Have not You, O God, cast us off? And You go not forth, O God, with our armies.
60:13 Give us help against the adversary, for vain is the help of man.
60:14 Through God we shall do valiantly; for He it is that will tread down our adversaries.

text.NRSV:
61:1 Hear my cry, O God; listen to my prayer.
61:2 From the end of the earth I call to you, when my heart is faint. Lead me to the rock that is higher than I;
61:3 for you are my refuge, a strong tower against the enemy.
61:4 Let me abide in your tent forever, find refuge under the shelter of your wings. Selah
61:5 For you, O God, have heard my vows; you have given me the heritage of those who fear your name.
61:6 Prolong the life of the king; may his years endure to all generations!
61:7 May he be enthroned in the presence of God forever; appoint steadfast love and faithfulness to watch over him!
61:8 So I will always sing praises to your name, as I pay my vows day after day.
text.Coverdale:
61:1 Hear my cry, O God, *
      and listen to my prayer.
61:2 I call upon you from the ends of the earth
      with heaviness in my heart; *
      set me upon the rock that is higher than I.
61:3 For you have been my refuge, *
      a strong tower against the enemy.
61:4 I will dwell in your house for ever; *
      I will take refuge under the cover of your wings.
61:5 For you, O God, have heard my vows; *
      you have granted me the heritage of those who fear your Name.
61:6 Add length of days to the king's life; *
      let his years extend over many generations.
61:7 Let him sit enthroned before God for ever; *
      bid love and faithfulness watch over him.
61:8 So will I always sing the praise of your Name, *
      and day by day I will fulfill my vows.
text.KJV:
61:1 Hear my cry, O God; attend unto my prayer.
61:2 From the end of the earth will I cry unto thee, when my heart is overwhelmed: lead me to the rock that is higher than I.
61:3 For thou hast been a shelter for me, and a strong tower from the enemy.
61:4 I will abide in thy tabernacle for ever: I will trust in the covert of thy wings. Selah.
61:5 For thou, O God, hast heard my vows: thou hast given me the heritage of those that fear thy name.
61:6 Thou wilt prolong the king's life: and his years as many generations.
61:7 He shall abide before God for ever: O prepare mercy and truth, which may preserve him.
61:8 So will I sing praise unto thy name for ever, that I may daily perform my vows.
text.JPS:
61:1 For the leader; with stringed instruments. Of David.
61:2 Hear my cry, O God; attend unto my prayer.
61:3 From the end of the earth I call unto You, when my heart is faint; lead me to a rock that is high for me.
61:4 For You have been a refuge for me, a tower of strength against the enemy.
61:5 I will sojourn in Your tent forever; I will take refuge in the covert of Your wings. Selah.
61:6 For You, O God, have heard my vows; You have given me the heritage of those who fear Your name.
61:7 May You add days unto the king's days! May his years be as many generations!
61:8 May he be enthroned before God forever! Appoint steadfast love and truth, that they may preserve him.
61:9 So will I sing praise unto Your name forever, that I may daily perform my vows.

text.NRSV:
62:1 For God alone my soul in silence waits; from him comes my salvation.
62:2 He alone is my rock and my salvation, my stronghold, so that I shall not be greatly shaken.
62:3 How long will you assail me to crush me, all of you together, as if you were a leaning wall, a tottering fence?
62:4 Their only plan is to bring me down from my height; they delight in lies; they bless with their mouth, but inwardly they curse.
62:5 For God alone my soul in silence waits; truly, my hope is in him.
62:6 He alone is my rock and my salvation, my stronghold, so that I shall not be shaken.
62:7 In God is my safety and my honor; God is my strong rock and my refuge.
62:8 Put your trust in him always, O people, pour out your hearts before him, for God is our refuge.
62:9 Those of high degree are but a fleeting breath, even those of low estate cannot be trusted.
62:10 On the scales they are lighter than a puff of wind, all of them together.
62:11 Put no trust in extortion; in robbery take no empty pride; though wealth increase, set not your heart upon it.
62:12 God has spoken once, twice have I heard it, that power belongs to God.
62:13 Steadfast love is yours, O Lord, for you repay everyone according to his deeds.

      all of you together, *
      as if you were a leaning fence, a toppling wall?

62:5 They bless with their lips, *

62:9 Put your trust in him always, O people, *

62:10 Those of high degree are but a fleeting breath, *
      even those of low estate cannot be trusted.
62:11 On the scales they are lighter than a breath, *
      all of them together.
62:12 Put no trust in extortion;
      in robbery take no empty pride; *
      though wealth increase, set not your heart upon it.
62:13 God has spoken once, twice have I heard it, *
      that power belongs to God.
62:14 Steadfast love is yours, O Lord, *
